elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Once the payment or payments have been received and the Mortgage becomes eligible,

elements.text: (d) Borrowers in a Trial Period Plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Borrower was in a Trial Period Plan prior to entering into a forbearance plan, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower may be re-evaluated for a new Trial Period Plan within 30 days prior to or upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: completion of the forbearance plan. The Servicer must not resume or restart the terms of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: previous Trial Period Plan prior to the start of the forbearance plan. Instead, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must evaluate the Borrower based on the status of the Mortgage at the time of the new
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluation. If the Borrower meets all eligibility requirements upon completion of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan and accepts a new Trial Period Plan offer, the Borrower will be required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: start a new Trial Period Plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For any subsequent modification submissions, the Trial Period Plan prior to the start of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan will not be considered a failed Trial Period Plan for a Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification evaluation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Forbearance plan termination
elements.label: text

elements.text: The forbearance plan must be terminated if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer determines that the Borrower has failed to meet the terms specified in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any of the eligibility criteria for the forbearance plan is no longer satisfied
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer becomes aware that the Borrower’s hardship is resolved; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower requests termination of the forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Other forbearance plan conditions and requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Notifying Freddie Mac of forbearance plans via EDR
elements.label: text

elements.text: At the end of the forbearance plan, the Servicer must work with the Borrower to cure the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency via a reinstatement or repayment plan, or by completing a workout option.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must evaluate the Borrower in accordance with the loss mitigation hierarchy
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Section 9201.2 and must also comply with the appropriate EDR reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirement to inform Freddie Mac about the solution entered into.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: If the Servicer enters into a repayment plan with the Borrower, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must report the repayment plan via EDR according to Section 9203.2(d).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower enters into a workout option that brings the account current, such as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: settled modification or payment deferral, the Servicer must no longer report the EDR
elements.label: text

elements.text: code for forbearance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For additional information about EDR, refer to Section 9102.6.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Forbearance plans for Mortgages subject to recourse
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages subject to recourse including but not limited to indemnification, Servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: are strongly encouraged, but not required, to consider eligible Borrowers for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan. Entering into a forbearance agreement (whether approved by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac or the Servicer) does not waive, delete, alter or supersede any recourse or
elements.label: text

elements.text: indemnification requirements set forth in the Servicer’s Purchase Documents and any
elements.label: text

elements.text: other documents containing recourse or indemnification obligations, and such recourse or
elements.label: text

elements.text: indemnification obligations will remain in full force and effect.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Credit reporting for Mortgages in a forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must continue to report a “full-file” status to the four major credit repositories
elements.label: text

elements.text: for each Mortgage in a forbearance plan in accordance with the Fair Credit Reporting Act
elements.label: text

elements.text: and credit bureau standards as provided by the Consumer Data Industry Association. See
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 51, Credit Repositories and Information to Report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Servicer policy and procedures for forbearance plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must have written forbearance plan policies and procedures describing how
elements.label: text

elements.text: to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Determine the Borrower’s hardship
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Determine whether to require a reduced payment and any payment amount; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Document the Servicer’s decision-making process, including, but not limited to, its
elements.label: text

elements.text: application of discretion or business judgement
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must consistently apply these policies and procedures.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9203.3: Forbearance plans and requirements (Future
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: effective date 10/01/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ What is a forbearance?
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requirements for a forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower contact requirements when transitioning from a forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrowers in a Trial Period Plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Forbearance plan termination
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other forbearance plan conditions and requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) What is a forbearance?
elements.label: text

elements.text: A forbearance plan is a written agreement between the Servicer and the Borrower (or
elements.label: text

elements.text: deceased Borrower’s estate) that reflects the terms of the forbearance, including whether the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower may make either reduced or no monthly payments for a specific period of time. If
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer achieves quality right party contact and the Borrower meets the eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: criteria for a forbearance plan in accordance with Section 9203.3(b), the Servicer must offer:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An initial forbearance term for a period of one to three months, and, if necessary
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ One or more forbearance term extensions of one to three months
elements.label: text

elements.text: The forbearance plan may not be extended beyond a date that would cause the Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: to exceed a cumulative total of 12 months of the Borrower’s contractual monthly Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment, including taxes and insurance, if the Servicer is collecting Escrow for those
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses, without prior approval from Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Prior to the expiration of the initial and any extension to the forbearance plan, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must evaluate the Borrower’s eligibility for an extension of the initial forbearance plan based
elements.label: text

elements.text: on quality right party contact. Each forbearance plan evaluation, including an evaluation for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan extension, must be completed in accordance with all eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in this section and Sections 9203.3(b) through 9203.3(f). Additionally, there is no
elements.label: text

elements.text: limit on the number of forbearance plans that an eligible Borrower can receive over the life
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 93, Evaluation Notices, includes forbearance agreements that a Servicer may use but
elements.label: text

elements.text: must amend as necessary to comply with the requirements of this section, Sections 9203.3(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: through 9203.3(f) and applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: At the end of the forbearance period, the Borrower must cure the Delinquency through one of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following options:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Full reinstatement of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Partial reinstatement followed by a repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Payment of the Mortgage in full
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A loan modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pay off the Mortgage through a short sale or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: A loan modification is a type of workout option that enables the Borrower to retain
elements.label: text

elements.text: homeownership (see Section 9201.6). If the Borrower can no longer afford to retain the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property, the Servicer must pursue a workout option to liquidate the Borrower’s interest in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the property (see Section 9201.6), such as a short sale, deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, a workout
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage assumption, or a simultaneous modification and assumption.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Workout options and Servicing Mortgages impacted by disaster requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic Guide location
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Flex Modification® Chapter 9206
elements.label: text

elements.text: Workout Mortgage assumption Chapter 9207
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Standard Short Sale Chapter 9208
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Standard Deed-in-Lieu of Foreclosure Chapter 9209
elements.label: text

elements.text: Charge-off Chapter 9210
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Mortgages impacted by a disaster Chapter 8404
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Requirements for a forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must use Resolve® to report forbearance plans to Freddie Mac. Servicers should
elements.label: text

elements.text: refer to Resolve Online Help for details on the use of the Resolve to report forbearance
elements.label: text

elements.text: plans.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: When forbearance plans are submitted via Resolve, the system will report the
elements.label: text

elements.text: associated EDR default action codes on behalf of the Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Borrower eligibility requirements for a forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be eligible for a forbearance plan, the Borrower:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must have an eligible hardship as described in Section 9202.1(b) and Form 710,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage Assistance Application. The Borrower’s hardship may be verbally stated.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must report the hardship reason via EDR in accordance with Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ May be either current or delinquent, but the forbearance plan must not result in an
elements.label: text

elements.text: overall Mortgage Delinquency that exceeds 360 days (i.e., to be in an active
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan, the Borrower must not have missed more than 12 contractual
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly Mortgage payments)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: No Borrower documentation or written application (e.g., Form 710 or a Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: customized equivalent of Form 710) is required from the Borrower; however, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must achieve quality right party contact in accordance with the requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9102.3(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Mortgage eligibility and exclusions for a forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages secured by the Borrower’s Primary Residence, even if vacant, are eligible for
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following Mortgages are ineligible for forbearance:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages secured by abandoned or condemned properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages secured by second homes or Investment Properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Forbearance plan agreement with the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must determine the terms of the forbearance agreement based on
elements.label: text

elements.text: discussions with the Borrower. The forbearance agreement:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must be provided to the Borrower in writing and signed by the Servicer. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: document does not have to be signed by the Borrower or returned to the Servicer as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: condition of the forbearance plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must indicate the duration of the forbearance plan, including the effective date, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: expiration date of the forbearance plan and, if applicable, the due date of the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment and the amount of such payment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 9203.3(b)(iv) for additional information about reduced payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: (if applicable) under a forbearance plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must state that, at the end of the forbearance period, the Borrower must either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Cure the Delinquency through a full reinstatement, partial reinstatement plus
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment plan or a repayment plan, or payoff of the Mortgage, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Submit a complete Borrower Response package to be evaluated for a workout
elements.label: text

elements.text: option prior to the conclusion of the plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Sections 9203.1(a) through 9203.2(d) for requirements on
elements.label: text

elements.text: reinstatements and repayment plans and to Chapters 9206 through 9210 for
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements on workout options.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must state that foreclosure proceedings are suspended during the forbearance period
elements.label: text

elements.text: so long as the Borrower complies with the forbearance agreement and that, unless
elements.label: text

elements.text: prohibited by applicable law, the Servicer may recommence foreclosure at the point it
elements.label: text

elements.text: was suspended if the Borrower defaults on the forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ May include a requirement to pay any accrued late charges due from the Borrower at
elements.label: text

elements.text: the time the Servicer entered into the forbearance agreement with the Borrower. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must not accrue or collect late charges from the Borrower during the
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance period, or any subsequent repayment plan period, if the Borrower is
elements.label: text

elements.text: complying with the terms of such agreements. If the Borrower defaults on the terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the forbearance agreement, late charge accrual may recommence from the date the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower defaulted on the agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Reduced payments under a forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a monthly payment is required under the terms of a forbearance plan:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Such payment must be less than the Borrower’s monthly contractual Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment, including, as applicable, principal, interest and Escrow payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must receive such payment from the Borrower on or before the last day
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the month in which it is due. If the Borrower fails to make timely forbearance
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments, the forbearance plan must be canceled unless the Servicer determines that
elements.label: text

elements.text: there are mitigating circumstances that caused the payment to be late.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If there are mitigating circumstances that prevents the Borrower from making the
elements.label: text

elements.text: required reduced payment, the Servicer may determine whether to amend the
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan to further reduce the required payment or allow the Borrower to make
elements.label: text

elements.text: no payments under a new forbearance plan agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower fails to make timely payments and there are no mitigating
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evaluate the Borrower for other alternatives to foreclosure (e.g., solicit the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: for a complete Borrower Response Package
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Conduct an evaluation for a streamlined offering of the Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification) and otherwise, pursue foreclosure in accordance with Chapter 9301.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Use good business judgment to determine whether forbearance payments were
elements.label: text

elements.text: received timely or if mitigating circumstances (such as a further reduction in income)
elements.label: text

elements.text: caused the payment to be late. Exceptions to the timely payment requirement must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: documented and retained in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Borrower contact requirements when transitioning from a forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Contact requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer has discretion to determine the appropriate frequency to contact the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower. However, the Servicer must initiate contact no later than 30 days prior to any
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan end date. Borrower contact must continue until quality right party
elements.label: text

elements.text: contact is achieved or until the forbearance plan has expired.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If quality right party contact is achieved, the Servicer must determine whether:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower’s hardship has been resolved
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower intends to remain in the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower must submit a complete Borrower Response Package to be evaluated
elements.label: text

elements.text: for other workout or liquidation options; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower is eligible for a streamlined modification offer for a Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification under Chapter 9206
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may use alternative outreach methods to contact the Borrower as permitted
elements.label: text

elements.text: by applicable law, including, but not limited to, e-mail, text messaging, voice response
elements.label: text

elements.text: unit technology or a Servicer’s web portal. In addition, the Servicer is authorized to mail,
elements.label: text

elements.text: fax or electronically transmit the Borrower Solicitation Package or its equivalent to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Post-forbearance plan – solicitations
elements.label: text

elements.text: In order to promote a more seamless transition between loss mitigation options when a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower who was on a forbearance completes the forbearance plan without a solution to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Delinquency, the table below provides requirements for Servicers to conduct reviews
elements.label: text

elements.text: for proactive Payment Deferral and Freddie Mac Flex Modification offers.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower must be eligible for a Payment Deferral in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of this chapter. However:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer is not required to have established quality right party contact, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Section 9203.4(c), and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer is not required to confirm that the Borrower meets the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility requirements described in Section 9203.4(c)(i)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Solicitation requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must solicit the Borrower using Exhibit 1102, Payment Deferral Post￾Forbearance Solicitation Cover Letter, with Exhibit 1100, Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agreement, or its equivalent, making any appropriate changes to comply with
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law. While use of Exhibits 1102 or 1100 is optional, it reflects the minimum
elements.label: text

elements.text: level of information that the Servicer must communicate and illustrates a level of
elements.label: text

elements.text: specificity that complies with the requirements of the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must include instruction on how to accept the offer in the Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Deferral agreement. The Servicer is authorized to consider the following as acceptance
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the Borrower, subject to applicable law:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower contacting the Servicer directly in accordance with any acceptable
elements.label: text

elements.text: outreach and communication method,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower returning an executed Payment Deferral agreement, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any other method evidencing the Borrower’s acceptance as determined by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower must make their full monthly contractual payment during the month of
elements.label: text

elements.text: solicitation and/ or during the processing month if, as of the date of evaluation:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is six months delinquent, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Payment Deferral would cause the Mortgage to exceed 12 months of
elements.label: text

elements.text: cumulative deferred past-due principal and interest payments resulting from
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment Deferrals
elements.label: text

elements.text: In these circumstances, the Servicer must complete the Payment Deferral within the
elements.label: text

elements.text: month of the solicitation or processing month after receipt of the Borrower’s full
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly contractual payment due during that month. While the current month’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment remains due because of delinquency and/or cumulative deferred payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements, the Servicer must send the proactive offer with the condition that a
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment or payments are required to be eligible to complete the Payment Deferral.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Once the payment or payments have been received and the Mortgage becomes eligible,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer can proceed to settlement.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: following the expiration of the forbearance
elements.label: text

elements.text: plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrowers forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ends, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must evaluate the Borrower for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: proactive offer for a Flex Modification, in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the eligibility criteria
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer has not established
elements.label: text

elements.text: quality right party contact to

elements.text: evaluate for a post-forbearance
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must evaluate the Borrower for eligibility for a proactive offer for a Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification in accordance with the requirements of Section 9206.1(c)(iii).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Solicitation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must send the Borrower Exhibit 1191 and the Exhibit 93 template for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Flex Modification Trial Period Plan Solicitation Offer – not based on an
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluation of a Borrower Response Package evaluation notice, or its equivalent.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibits 1191 and 93 may be altered at the Servicer’s discretion as it deems necessary
elements.label: text

elements.text: to meet the requirements of this section and Chapter 9206 and to comply with
elements.label: text

elements.text: disclosure and other requirements under applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Borrowers in a Trial Period Plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Borrower was in a Trial Period Plan prior to entering into a forbearance plan, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower may be re-evaluated for a new Trial Period Plan within 30 days prior to or upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: completion of the forbearance plan. The Servicer must not resume or restart the terms of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: previous Trial Period Plan prior to the start of the forbearance plan. Instead, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must evaluate the Borrower based on the status of the Mortgage at the time of the new
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluation. If the Borrower meets all eligibility requirements upon completion of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan and accepts a new Trial Period Plan offer, the Borrower will be required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: start a new Trial Period Plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For any subsequent modification submissions, the Trial Period Plan prior to the start of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan will not be considered a failed Trial Period Plan for a Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification evaluation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Forbearance plan termination
elements.label: text

elements.text: The forbearance plan must be terminated if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer determines that the Borrower has failed to meet the terms specified in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any of the eligibility criteria for the forbearance plan is no longer satisfied
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer becomes aware that the Borrower’s hardship is resolved; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower requests termination of the forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Other forbearance plan conditions and requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Reporting forbearance plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must report forbearance plans in Resolve, in accordance with criteria outlined
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Resolve Online Help, no later than the last day of the month in which a forbearance
elements.label: text

elements.text: plan starts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Forbearance plans for Mortgages subject to recourse
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages subject to recourse including but not limited to indemnification, Servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: are strongly encouraged, but not required, to consider eligible Borrowers for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan. Entering into a forbearance agreement (whether approved by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac or the Servicer) does not waive, delete, alter or supersede any recourse or
elements.label: text

elements.text: indemnification requirements set forth in the Servicer’s Purchase Documents and any
elements.label: text

elements.text: other documents containing recourse or indemnification obligations, and such recourse or
elements.label: text

elements.text: indemnification obligations will remain in full force and effect.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Credit reporting for Mortgages in a forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must continue to report a “full-file” status to the four major credit repositories
elements.label: text

elements.text: for each Mortgage in a forbearance plan in accordance with the Fair Credit Reporting Act
elements.label: text

elements.text: and credit bureau standards as provided by the Consumer Data Industry Association. See
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 51, Credit Repositories and Information to Report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Servicer policy and procedures for forbearance plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must have written forbearance plan policies and procedures describing how
elements.label: text

elements.text: to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Determine the Borrower’s hardship
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Determine whether to require a reduced payment and any payment amount; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Document the Servicer’s decision-making process, including, but not limited to, its
elements.label: text

elements.text: application of discretion or business judgement
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must consistently apply these policies and procedures.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9203.4: Payment Deferral eligibility, processing,
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: conditions and requirements (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ What is a Payment Deferral?
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer approval authority for Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligibility requirements for a Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligibility exclusions for a Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Determining the terms of a Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Completing Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Submission of Payment Deferral terms and settlement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other payment deferral conditions and requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Disaster Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Electronic Payment Deferral Agreements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) What is a Payment Deferral?
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Payment Deferral is a relief option to assist a Borrower who is at least 60 days but less
elements.label: text

elements.text: than or equal to 180 days delinquent to cure the Delinquency by deferring the delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: principal and interest amounts of the contractual monthly Mortgage payment(s) and eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: advances into a newly created or an existing non-interest bearing UPB. Those amounts will
elements.label: text

elements.text: become due and payable at the earlier of the Mortgage maturity date, payoff date or transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: or sale of the Mortgaged Premises. All other terms of the existing Mortgage must remain
elements.label: text

elements.text: unchanged.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Servicer approval authority for Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must evaluate the Borrower for a Payment Deferral under Sections 9203.4(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: through 9203.4(h) in accordance with the evaluation hierarchy in Sections 9201.2 and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless otherwise notified by Freddie Mac, all Servicers are delegated to approve and offer a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment Deferral to eligible Borrowers in accordance with the requirements of the Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: and other applicable Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Eligibility requirements for a Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must establish achieve quality right party contact with the Borrower in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements described in Section 9102.3(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Borrower eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the information required to achieve quality right party contact, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must confirm that the Borrower:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Has a resolved hardship
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Has the financial capacity to continue making the existing contractual monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage payment, including amounts due as a result of an Escrow shortage, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Is unable to afford a repayment plan or full reinstatement of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Mortgage and property eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must be at least 60 days delinquent but less than or equal to 180 days delinquent as of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the evaluation date for the Payment Deferral.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 9203.4(g)(ii) for processing instructions when additional
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments may be required during the month of evaluation and/or processing month.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must have been originated at least 12 months prior to the evaluation date for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must be a conventional First Lien Mortgage currently owned or guaranteed by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ May be a fixed-rate Mortgage, ARM or Step-Rate Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: The property may be a Primary Residence, second home or Investment Property and
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be vacant or condemned.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Borrower documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer is not required to collect a complete Borrower Response Package to
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluate the Borrower for a Payment Deferral.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower submits a complete Borrower Response Package, the Servicer:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must evaluate the Borrower in accordance with the requirements for reviewing and
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluating a complete Borrower Response Package as specified in the Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Is authorized to use an Evaluation Notice (refer to Exhibit 93, Evaluation Notices) in
elements.label: text

elements.text: response to a complete Borrower Response Package and must make the appropriate
elements.label: text

elements.text: changes to reflect the terms of the Payment Deferral. For those Payment Deferrals
elements.label: text

elements.text: offered without a complete Borrower Response Package, the Servicer is not required
elements.label: text

elements.text: to use an Evaluation Notice in addition to the Payment Deferral agreement but may
elements.label: text

elements.text: do so at their discretion.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Mortgages subject to indemnification agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage is subject to an indemnification agreement and is otherwise eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the Payment Deferral requirements in Sections 9203.4(a) through 9203.4(h), the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer has the discretion to approve the Payment Deferral provided the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: conditions are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage receiving the Payment Deferral retains its credit enhancement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Servicer is not the credit enhancement provider, the Servicer must first obtain in
elements.label: text

elements.text: writing any required approval under the terms of the credit enhancement from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: entity providing the enhancement to enter into a Payment Deferral that complies with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirements of Sections 9203.4(a) through 9203.4(h)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer remits to Freddie Mac an annual payment for the amount of all Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Deferral-related costs (e.g., interest rate shortfall). The loss amount calculations for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Payment Deferral will be determined by Freddie Mac in the same manner as the
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculations for modification loss amounts. The Payment Deferral loss amounts due
elements.label: text

elements.text: will be calculated on a monthly basis and billed on an annual basis for the life of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage that is subject to a Payment Deferral.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage is subject to a partial indemnification, each year the Servicer will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: billed the appropriate percentage of the Payment Deferral loss amount that
elements.label: text

elements.text: corresponds with the partial indemnification agreement. The Payment Deferral loss
elements.label: text

elements.text: amounts will be determined by Freddie Mac in accordance with a process described
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Bulletins 2021-14 and 2021-31.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The Servicer is not eligible for an incentive for completing a Payment Deferral on
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Mortgage that is subject to an indemnification agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Mortgage insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage is subject to mortgage insurance and approval of the Payment Deferral is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not covered by a delegation agreement, the Servicer must obtain delegation of authority
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the MI or seek approval from the MI to complete the Payment Deferral.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi) Texas Equity Section 50(a)(6) Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower is eligible and qualifies for a Payment Deferral, the Servicer must offer
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Payment Deferral to the Borrower. If the Servicer receives Borrower notification
elements.label: text

elements.text: classifying the Payment Deferral as a loan modification and claiming that the terms of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification agreement do not comply with the provisions of Article XVI Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 50(a)(6) of the Texas Constitution, the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac within seven
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Days of receipt of such objection or complaint via Freddie Mac Servicing Data
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corrections and include the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac loan number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer loan number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transaction type (e.g., Texas Home Equity modification)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accounting Cycle in which Freddie Mac settled the workout
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer’s analysis (e.g., Borrower complaint related to Section 50(a)(6) of the Texas
elements.label: text

elements.text: Constitution)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon receipt of Freddie Mac’s instructions, the Servicer must comply with any required
elements.label: text

elements.text: response time frames to claims of defects and any other complaint in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 8104.1(a) and the Texas Constitution.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Eligibility exclusions for a Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following Mortgages and Borrowers are ineligible for a Payment Deferral:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ FHA, VA and Guaranteed Rural Housing Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages subject to recourse
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages that have received a prior non-disaster related Payment Deferral with an
elements.label: text

elements.text: effective date within 12 months of the evaluation date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrowers who, within the 12 months prior to the evaluation date for a Payment Deferral,
elements.label: text

elements.text: failed a non-disaster related modification Trial Period Plan (e.g., Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification Trial Period Plan and the terms of that Trial Period Plan were determined in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 9206.2(a))
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Converting from a modification Trial Period Plan to a forbearance plan, regardless
elements.label: text

elements.text: of whether subject to an Eligible Disaster or not, is not considered to be a failed Trial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Period Plan modification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages previously modified under the Freddie Mac Flex Modification®, or other non￾disaster related modification, where the Modification Effective Date is within the
elements.label: text

elements.text: previous 12 months of the evaluation date for a Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages that are subject to an approved short sale or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages that are currently subject to an unexpired offer to the Borrower for another
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage modification or repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrowers who are currently performing under another modification Trial Period Plan or
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages for which the Payment Deferral, if completed, would result in a cumulative
elements.label: text

elements.text: total of more than 12 months of deferred principal and interest payments, including
elements.label: text

elements.text: amounts deferred as a result of previous non-disaster related Payment Deferrals
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages with a maturity date that is within 36 months of the evaluation date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages with a projected payoff date based on payments due under the existing
elements.label: text

elements.text: amortization schedule that is within 36 months of the evaluation date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the Mortgage is within 36 months of its maturity date or projected payoff date
elements.label: text

elements.text: but the Servicer determines based on the Borrower’s individual circumstances that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower should be considered for a Payment Deferral, the Servicer may transmit an
elements.label: text

elements.text: exception request to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Determining the terms of a Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Servicer requirements for determining the terms of a Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must follow the steps below when determining the terms of the Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Deferral. If the existing Mortgage includes a non-interest bearing UPB as a result of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior modification, the terms impacting that non-interest bearing UPB will remain
elements.label: text

elements.text: unchanged.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must apply the Payment Deferral forbearance in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following steps:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Deferring delinquent amounts. Defer at least 2 months and up to 6 months of past￾due principal and interest payment(s) and any other expenses or amounts that are
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitted to be capitalized under the Freddie Mac Flex Modification capitalization
elements.label: text

elements.text: rules in Section 9206.3(b)(ii) into an existing or newly created non-interest bearing
elements.label: text

elements.text: UPB (i.e., deferred UPB). The deferred UPB will become due on the earlier of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage maturity date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage payoff date (e.g., refinance or payoff of the interest-bearing UPB);
elements.label: text

elements.text: or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Upon transfer or sale of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Reporting the Payment Deferral. The Servicer must advance the DDLPI in order to
elements.label: text

elements.text: bring the Mortgage to current status.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Remaining payment schedule. Ensure the remaining payment schedule associated
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the interest-bearing UPB remains unchanged, from the Mortgage’s payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: schedule before applying the Payment Deferral.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Late charges. The Servicer must waive all accrued and unpaid late charges upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: completion of the Payment Deferral.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Remaining Mortgage terms. When offering the Payment Deferral, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: ensure all other remaining terms of the existing Mortgage remain unchanged
elements.label: text

elements.text: including, but not limited to the:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Remaining amortization schedule
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monthly P&I portion of the existing contractual monthly Mortgage payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Interest rate (including maintaining the existing rate adjustment schedule for an
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARM or a Step-Rate Mortgage)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maturity date; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Due dates of the remaining payment due under the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: The maximum number of cumulative Principal and Interest Payments that may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: deferred for a Mortgage subject to non-disaster Payment Deferrals over the life of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is 12.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: Prior to or during the Servicer’s determination of the Borrower’s eligibility for a Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Deferral, the Servicer must analyze the Borrower’s Escrow to estimate if there is an
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escrow shortage. If the Servicer completed an estimated analysis during evaluation, then
elements.label: text

elements.text: upon completion of the Payment Deferral, the Servicer must perform a new Escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: analysis in accordance with the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act (RESPA) and any
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable federal, State or local laws.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If, as a result of the analysis, the Servicer determines that there is an Escrow shortage, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must disclose the amount of the shortage to the Borrower before the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: accepts the Payment Deferral. If the Borrower is unable to pay the Escrow shortage as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: lump sum, then the Borrower must pay the shortage as part of the monthly principal,
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest, taxes and insurance (PITI) payment. If the Borrower must make monthly Escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: shortage payments, then the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Spread the repayment of the Escrow shortage amount in equal monthly payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: over a period of 60 months, unless the Borrower chooses to pay off the shortage over
elements.label: text

elements.text: a shorter period of time, not to be less than 12 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Take into account any remaining unpaid amount of the Escrow shortage in any
elements.label: text

elements.text: subsequent Escrow analysis to ensure that the Borrower is able to continue to pay all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escrow shortage amounts over the remaining portion of either the current remaining
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escrow shortage repayment period or a period up to 60 months. The Servicer may not
elements.label: text

elements.text: accelerate or compress the remaining Escrow shortage amount into a new Escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment or shorter repayment period as a result of a future Escrow analysis.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower is unable to afford a Payment Deferral based on the increased monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment resulting from an Escrow shortage repayment, the Servicer must evaluate the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To complete a Payment Deferral, the Servicer must perform an actual Escrow analysis (as
elements.label: text

elements.text: opposed to an estimate) in accordance with RESPA and any applicable federal, State or
elements.label: text

elements.text: local law upon completion of the Payment Deferral and, if applicable, establish a
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment plan in accordance with the above requirements. Any Escrow shortage that is
elements.label: text

elements.text: identified at the time of the Payment Deferral must not be deferred to the non-interest￾bearing UPB, and the Servicer is not required to fund any existing Escrow shortage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer is not required to revoke a previous waiver of Escrow, if applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Completing Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must complete the Payment Deferral in the same month it determines the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower is eligible. If the Servicer is unable to complete the Payment Deferral within the
elements.label: text

elements.text: same month of its determination, the Servicer may, at its option and in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: “processing month” requirements below, use an additional month to allow for sufficient
elements.label: text

elements.text: processing time (“processing month”) to complete the Payment Deferral.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Processing month requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer is unable to complete (i.e., submit the case via Freddie Mac’s servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: system) the Payment Deferral before the 15th day of the evaluation month, then the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer is authorized to use an additional month to allow for sufficient processing time
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a “processing month”) to complete a Payment Deferral. The Servicer must treat all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers equally in applying the processing month, as evidenced by a written policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i.e., the criteria for requiring a processing month must be the same for all Borrowers).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, the Servicer is not permitted to defer more than 6 months of principal and
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest payments as part of a Payment Deferral or to exceed 12 months of cumulative
elements.label: text

elements.text: deferred principal and interest payments from all Payment Deferrals completed on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage. As a result, the Borrower must make their full monthly contractual payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: during the processing month if, as of the evaluation date:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is already 180 days delinquent, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Payment Deferral would cause the Mortgage to exceed 12 months of cumulative
elements.label: text

elements.text: deferred past-due Principal and Interest Payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: While the current month’s payment remains due because of delinquency and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: cumulative deferred payment requirements, the Servicer must send the proactive offer
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the condition that a payment or payments are required for the Borrower to become
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible to complete the Payment Deferral. Once the payment or payments have been
elements.label: text

elements.text: received and the Mortgage becomes eligible, the Servicer can proceed to settlement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Otherwise, the Borrower is not required to submit a payment during the processing month
elements.label: text

elements.text: for a Payment Deferral.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Payment Deferral agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must send Exhibit 1100, Payment Deferral Agreement, or equivalent to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower no later than five days after the completion (i.e., a settled workout option) of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Payment Deferral. If the Servicer elects to require the Borrower to sign and return the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment Deferral agreement, it must receive the fully executed Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement prior to the settlement date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Use of Exhibit 1100 is optional. It reflects the minimum level of information that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must communicate to the Borrower, and it illustrates a level of specificity that is
elements.label: text

elements.text: deemed to be in compliance with the requirements of the Guide. The Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: ensure the Payment Deferral agreement complies with applicable federal, State or local
elements.label: text

elements.text: laws.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When processing a Payment Deferral agreement, the Servicer must also comply with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Maintaining lien status
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer’s application of a Payment Deferral to the Mortgage must not impair
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s First Lien position or enforceability against the Borrower(s) in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with its terms.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Title endorsement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Title endorsement is not required.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Document Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: After the Servicer has sent the executed Payment Deferral agreement to the Borrower in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements in this section, the Servicer must comply with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Payment Deferral agreement is not required to be signed by the Borrower, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must send a copy of the Servicer executed Payment Deferral agreement to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Document Custodian within 25 days of the effective date of the Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: completion
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Payment Deferral agreement is to be recorded, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text:  Send a certified copy of the fully executed Payment Deferral agreement to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian within 25 days of the effective date of the Payment Deferral;
elements.label: text

elements.text: and
elements.label: text

elements.text:  Send the original Payment Deferral agreement that is returned from the recorder’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: office to the Document Custodian within five Business Days of receipt
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Payment Deferral agreement must be signed by the Borrower but not recorded,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must send the fully executed original Payment Deferral agreement to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian within 25 days of the effective date of the Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Submission of Payment Deferral terms and settlement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment Deferrals can be processed and settled daily at Freddie Mac, except on the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Day of the month. Servicers can monitor the settlement status of all Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Deferrals daily via the Modification Overview Report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must use Resolve® for all Mortgages for which the Borrowers are being evaluated
elements.label: text

elements.text: for a Payment Deferral under this chapter. Resolve requires the submission of specific data
elements.label: text

elements.text: elements in order to return a suggested workout solution. Based on the information input by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer, Resolve will determine the terms of the Payment Deferral. Servicers and any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Users that use or access Resolve are bound by all of the provisions of the Master System
elements.label: text

elements.text: License (see Section 2401.1) and the System-Specific License for Servicing Tools (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 2404.2). Each Servicer shall use Resolve “in accordance with (A) the requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: this section, (B) the Payment Deferral and other instructions provided in Resolve Online
elements.label: text

elements.text: Help and any other Documentation, and (C) any other applicable provisions of the Guide,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including Sections 2401.1 and 2404.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Instant settlement via Resolve
elements.label: text

elements.text: To process the Payment Deferral for immediate settlement, the requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 9203.4(a) and 9203.4(h) must be met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To complete the settlement of the Payment Deferral, the Servicer must submit the
elements.label: text

elements.text: settlement request in Resolve.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Payment Deferrals not eligible for instant settlement via Resolve
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the settlement request is submitted but the workout is not eligible for instant settlement,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer is expected to follow the response provided by Resolve and correct any
elements.label: text

elements.text: issues that were highlighted in Resolve’s response and submit for settlement accordingly.
elements.label: text

elements.text: As an example, a Borrower is not immediately eligible if, as of the evaluation date,
elements.label: text

elements.text: either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is 180 days delinquent or greater, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Payment Deferral would cause the Mortgage to exceed 12 months of cumulative
elements.label: text

elements.text: deferred past-due principal and interest payments resulting from Payment Deferrals
elements.label: text

elements.text: In these circumstances, the Borrower may become eligible upon subsequent receipt of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment or payments that are due. In such instances, the Servicer can proceed to settling
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Payment Deferral once the Borrower becomes eligible upon payment receipt. This
elements.label: text

elements.text: may occur as a result of proactive solicitations required by Section 9203.3(c)(ii) or in
elements.label: text

elements.text: other instances where a Borrower who is ineligible as a result of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency and/or the cumulative number of payments deferred subsequently makes a
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment or payments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: By transmitting the data to Freddie Mac via Resolve for settlement, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: represents and warrants that it has complied with all applicable requirements in the Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: and applicable Purchase Documents governing:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer’s delegated authority to process a Mortgage subject to the terms of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment Deferral, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer’s use of Resolve to complete the settlement
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Other Payment Deferral conditions and requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Delinquency status reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Payment Deferral does not have an associated unique EDR status code. For each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage subject to the Payment Deferral, the Servicer must continue reporting the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate delinquency status information to Freddie Mac through the EDR Tool in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with requirements in Section 9102.6 and Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools. After
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Payment Deferral has been completed and the Mortgage is brought current the EDR
elements.label: text

elements.text: status code must reflect the Mortgage as current.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Borrower redefaults and enters a new forbearance plan, the Servicer must report the
elements.label: text

elements.text: new plan through the EDR tool in accordance with the requirements described above.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Credit reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each Mortgage that is subject to the Payment Deferral, the Servicer must provide a
elements.label: text

elements.text: “full file” status report describing the status of the Mortgage to each of the four major
elements.label: text

elements.text: credit repositories in accordance with the credit bureau standards as provided by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Consumer Data Industry Association.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Incentive payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer is eligible to receive a $500 incentive payment for each completed Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Deferral.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Servicing fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer will continue to receive the Servicing fee it was receiving prior to
elements.label: text

elements.text: completing the Payment Deferral.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi)Future Freddie Mac Flex Modification evaluations
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer is evaluating a Borrower for a future Freddie Mac Flex Modification, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment Deferral will not count as a previous loan modification for purposes of
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculating the number of times the Mortgage has previously been modified.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (viii) Additional requirements for Mortgages with buydown funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: The additional requirements for Mortgages with buydown funds are as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When processing a Payment Deferral for Mortgages subject to a temporary subsidy
elements.label: text

elements.text: buydown plan in accordance with Section 4204.3, the Servicer must not apply funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: in a buydown account to reduce the total amount to be deferred in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirements of a Payment Deferral as specified in Section 9203.4(e)(i) unless it
elements.label: text

elements.text: is required under the terms of the applicable buydown agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When evaluating a Borrower with a Mortgage with a temporary subsidy buydown
elements.label: text

elements.text: plan for a Payment Deferral, the Servicer must confirm the Borrower’s financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: capacity to continue making the existing contractual monthly Mortgage payment,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including amounts due as a result of an Escrow shortage, as specified in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Upon completion of the Payment Deferral, the Borrower must resume their payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount as specified in the terms of the buydown agreement and in subject to
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law*
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Buydown term has expired or if, for any reason, the buydown funds are not
elements.label: text

elements.text: available or the buydown funds are not paid, the Borrower is responsible to continue
elements.label: text

elements.text: to make full monthly Mortgage payments as required by the terms of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any remaining funds held in association with a buydown account, upon the end of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: buydown term or the liquidation of the Mortgage, whichever occurs first, the Servicer

elements.text: terms of the buydown agreement, applicable law and the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Disaster Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Disaster Payment Deferral is a relief option to assist Borrowers who were impacted and
elements.label: text

elements.text: became delinquent due to an Eligible Disaster as defined in Section 8404.1. The Disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment Deferral is designed to cure the Delinquency by deferring the delinquent principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: and interest amounts of the contractual monthly Mortgage payment(s) into a newly created or
elements.label: text

elements.text: an existing non-interest bearing UPB. Those amounts will become due and payable at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: earlier of the Mortgage maturity date, payoff date or transfer or sale of the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises. All other terms of the existing Mortgage must remain unchanged.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless otherwise notified by Freddie Mac, all Freddie Mac Servicers are delegated to
elements.label: text

elements.text: approve and offer a Freddie Mac Disaster Payment Deferral to eligible Borrowers in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements of the Guide and other applicable Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Eligibility requirements and exclusions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must achieve quality right party contact with the Borrower in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the requirements described in Section 9102.3(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Borrower eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the information required to achieve quality right party contact, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must confirm that the Borrower:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Has a resolved hardship
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Is capable of continuing to make the existing contractual monthly Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Is unable to afford a repayment plan or full reinstatement of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Delinquency and payment requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have been current or less than 60 days delinquent (i.e., less than two months
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent) as of the date of the Eligible Disaster, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be at least 30 days delinquent (i.e., one month) but less than or equal to 360 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent (i.e., 12 months) as of the date of evaluation. (Refer to Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9203.4(g)(ii) for processing instructions when additional payments may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: required during the month of evaluation and/or processing month.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If a Borrower’s hardship is the result of an Eligible Disaster but the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: was 60 or more days delinquent as of the date of the disaster and the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: determines the Borrower can maintain the existing monthly contractual Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment, the Servicer must submit an exception request via Resolve to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (C)Mortgage and property eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must be a conventional First Lien Mortgage currently owned or guaranteed by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ May be a fixed-rate Mortgage, ARM or Step-Rate Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: The property may be a Primary Residence, second home or Investment Property and
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be vacant or condemned.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (D)Borrower documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must not require a complete Borrower Response Package to evaluate the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower for a Disaster Payment Deferral if the Borrower has been evaluated in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with all requirements in the Guide and the eligibility requirements are
elements.label: text

elements.text: satisfied.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (E) Eligibility exclusions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following Mortgages and Borrowers are ineligible for the Disaster Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Deferral:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ FHA, VA and Guaranteed Rural Housing Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages subject to recourse
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Mortgage subject to a previous Disaster Payment Deferral related to the same
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligible Disaster event
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages subject to an approved short sale or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Mortgage currently subject to an unexpired offer to the Borrower for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage modification or repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrowers currently performing under a modification Trial Period Plan or
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Mortgage with a maturity date that is within 36 months of the evaluation date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Mortgage with a projected payoff date based on payments due under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: existing amortization schedule that is within 36 months of the evaluation date
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the Mortgage is within 36 months of its maturity date or projected payoff
elements.label: text

elements.text: date but the Servicer determines based on the Borrower’s individual circumstances
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the Borrower should be considered for a Disaster Payment Deferral, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: may transmit an exception request to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (F) Mortgages subject to indemnification agreements
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage is subject to an indemnification agreement and is otherwise eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the Disaster Payment Deferral requirements in this section, the Servicer has the
elements.label: text

elements.text: discretion to approve the Disaster Payment Deferral provided the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: conditions are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage receiving the Disaster Payment Deferral retains its credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: enhancement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer remits to Freddie Mac an annual payment for the amount of all

elements.label: text

elements.label: text
