elements.text: (H)Texas Equity Section 50(a)(6) Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower is eligible and qualifies for a Disaster Payment Deferral, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must offer the Disaster Payment Deferral to the Borrower. If the Servicer receives
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower notification classifying the Disaster Payment Deferral as a loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification and claiming that the terms of the modification agreement do not
elements.label: text

elements.text: comply with the provisions of Article XVI Section 50(a)(6) of the Texas Constitution,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac within seven Business Days of receipt of such
elements.label: text

elements.text: objection or complaint via Freddie Mac Servicing Data Corrections and include the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac loan number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer loan number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transaction type (e.g., Texas Home Equity modification)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accounting Cycle in which Freddie Mac settled the workout
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer’s analysis (e.g., Borrower complaint related to Section 50(a)(6) of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Texas Constitution)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon receipt of Freddie Mac’s instructions, the Servicer must comply with any
elements.label: text

elements.text: required response time frames to claims of defects and any other complaint in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 8104.1(a) and the Texas Constitution.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Determining Disaster Payment Deferral terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: Based on the information provided by the Servicer, Resolve will determine the terms of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Disaster Payment Deferral as described in this Section 9203.4(i)(ii).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the existing Mortgage includes a non-interest bearing UPB as a result of a prior
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification, the terms impacting that non-interest bearing UPB will remain unchanged.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Apply the Payment Deferral forbearance as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Defer the delinquent principal and interest and any other expenses or amounts that are
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitted to be capitalized under the Freddie Mac Flex Modification capitalization
elements.label: text

elements.text: rules in Section 9206.3(b)(ii) into an existing or newly created non-interest bearing
elements.label: text

elements.text: UPB (i.e., deferred UPB). The deferred UPB will become due on the earlier of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgage maturity date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgage payoff date (e.g., refinance or payoff of the interest-bearing UPB),
elements.label: text

elements.text: or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The transfer or sale of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Advance the DDLPI to bring the Mortgage to current status
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure the remaining payment schedule associated with the interest-bearing UPB
elements.label: text

elements.text: remains unchanged from the Mortgage’s pre-Disaster Payment Deferral payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: schedule
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Waive all accrued and unpaid late charges upon completion of the Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: When offering the Payment Deferral, the Servicer must ensure all other remaining terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the existing Mortgage remain unchanged, including, but not limited to the:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Remaining amortization schedule
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monthly P&I portion of the existing contractual monthly Mortgage payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Interest rate, including maintaining the existing rate adjustment schedule for an ARM
elements.label: text

elements.text: or a Step-Rate Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maturity date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Due Dates of the remaining payment due under the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The maximum number of monthly payments that may be deferred as part of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Disaster Payment Deferral is 12.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Prior to or during the Servicer’s determination of the Borrower’s eligibility for a Disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment Deferral, the Servicer must analyze the Borrower’s Escrow to estimate if there
elements.label: text

elements.text: is an Escrow shortage. If the Servicer completed an estimated analysis during evaluation,
elements.label: text

elements.text: then upon completion of the Disaster Payment Deferral, the Servicer must perform a new
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escrow analysis in accordance with the RESPA and any applicable federal, State or local
elements.label: text

elements.text: law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If, as a result of the analysis, the Servicer determines that there is an Escrow shortage, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must disclose the amount of the shortage to the Borrower before the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: accepts the Disaster Payment Deferral. If the Borrower is unable to pay the Escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: shortage as a lump sum, then the Borrower must pay the shortage as part of the monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: PITI payment. If the Borrower must make monthly Escrow shortage payments, then the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Spread the repayment of the Escrow shortage amount in equal monthly payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: over a period of 60 months, unless the Borrower chooses to pay off the shortage over
elements.label: text

elements.text: a shorter period of time (not to be less than 12 months)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Take into account any remaining unpaid amount of the Escrow shortage in any
elements.label: text

elements.text: subsequent Escrow analysis to ensure that the Borrower is able to continue to pay all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escrow shortage amounts over the remaining portion of either the current remaining
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escrow shortage repayment period or a period up to 60 months. The Servicer may not
elements.label: text

elements.text: accelerate or compress the remaining Escrow shortage amount into a new Escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment or shorter repayment period as a result of a future Escrow analysis.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower is unable to afford a Disaster Payment deferral based on the increased
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly payment resulting from an Escrow shortage repayment, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluate the Borrower for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any Escrow account shortage that is identified at the time of the Payment Deferral must
elements.label: text

elements.text: not be capitalized, and the Servicer is not required to fund any existing Escrow account
elements.label: text

elements.text: shortage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any Escrow advances must be included in the deferred balance, as described in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Delinquent Disaster Payment Deferral” section above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, the Servicer is not required to revoke any Escrow account waiver.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Completing a Disaster Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must send a Payment Deferral agreement (Exhibit 1100 or the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: customized equivalent of the Payment Deferral agreement) to the Borrower no later than
elements.label: text

elements.text: five days after completion (e.g., a settled workout option) of the Payment Deferral. If the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer elects to require the Borrower to sign and return the Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement, it must receive the fully executed Payment Deferral agreement prior to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: settlement date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Use of the Payment Deferral agreement is optional. It reflects the minimum level of
elements.label: text

elements.text: information that the Servicer must communicate to the Borrower, and it illustrates a level
elements.label: text

elements.text: of specificity that is deemed to be in compliance with the requirements of the Guide. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must ensure the Payment Deferral agreement complies with applicable federal,
elements.label: text

elements.text: State or local law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When processing a Payment Deferral agreement, the Servicer must also comply with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Maintaining lien status
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer’s application of a Disaster Payment Deferral to the Mortgage must not
elements.label: text

elements.text: impair Freddie Mac’s First Lien position or enforceability against the Borrower(s) in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with its terms.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Title endorsement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Title endorsement is not required.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (C)Document Custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: After the Servicer has sent the executed Payment Deferral agreement to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: as required in this section:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Payment Deferral agreement is not required to be signed by the Borrower,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must send a copy of the Servicer-executed Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement to the Document Custodian within 25 days of the effective date of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment Deferral completion
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Payment Deferral agreement is to be recorded, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text:  Send a certified copy of the fully executed Payment Deferral agreement to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian within 25 days of the effective date of the Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Deferral, and
elements.label: text

elements.text:  Send the original Payment Deferral agreement that is returned from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: recorder’s office to the Document Custodian within five Business Days of
elements.label: text

elements.text: receipt
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Payment Deferral agreement must be signed by the Borrower but not
elements.label: text

elements.text: recorded, the Servicer must send the fully executed original Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement to the Document Custodian within 25 days of the effective date of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Disaster Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Processing month
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must complete the Disaster Payment Deferral in the same month it
elements.label: text

elements.text: determines the Borrower is eligible. If the Servicer is unable to complete the Disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment Deferral before the 15th day of the evaluation month, then the Servicer is
elements.label: text

elements.text: authorized to use an additional month to allow for sufficient processing time (a
elements.label: text

elements.text: “processing month”) to complete the Disaster Payment Deferral. The Servicer must treat
elements.label: text

elements.text: all Borrowers equally in applying the processing month, as evidenced by a written policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i.e., the criteria for requiring a processing month must be the same for all Borrowers).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower must make a complete monthly contractual payment during the processing
elements.label: text

elements.text: month if, as of the date of evaluation, the Mortgage is 360 days delinquent or more. In
elements.label: text

elements.text: this circumstance, the Servicer must complete the Disaster Payment Deferral within the
elements.label: text

elements.text: processing month after the receipt of the Borrower’s full monthly contractual payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: due during that month. While the current month’s payment remains due because of
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquency and/or cumulative deferred payment requirements, the Servicer must send
elements.label: text

elements.text: the proactive offer with the condition that a payment or payments are required to become
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible to complete the Payment Deferral. Once the payment or payments have been
elements.label: text

elements.text: received and the Mortgage becomes eligible, the Servicer can proceed to settlement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Disaster Payment Deferral agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must process a Disaster Payment Deferral agreement in compliance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for processing a regular Payment Deferral agreement, as described in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9203.4(f).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi)Evaluation hierarchy
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be eligible for a Disaster Payment Deferral, a Borrower must have been current or less
elements.label: text

elements.text: than 60 days delinquent (i.e., less than two months delinquent) as of the date of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligible Disaster. Otherwise, the Servicer must conduct all loss mitigation evaluations in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Freddie Mac’s standard loss mitigation evaluation hierarchy, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Section 9201.2, or must submit an exception request for Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If quality right party contact is established with a Borrower who was current or less than
elements.label: text

elements.text: 60 days delinquent (i.e., less than two months delinquent) as of the date of the Eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: Disaster and the Borrower is unable to resolve the Delinquency through a reinstatement
elements.label: text

elements.text: or repayment plan, the Servicer must evaluate the Borrower for the loss mitigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: options in the following Disaster evaluation hierarchy:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Disaster Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Freddie Mac Flex Modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Standard Short Sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Standard Deed-in-Lieu of Foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: In most cases, Borrowers impacted by an Eligible Disaster who qualify to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluated for a Disaster Payment Deferral will be transitioning from a forbearance plan,
elements.label: text

elements.text: but forbearance is not a prerequisite.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (xi)Resolve
elements.label: text

elements.text: To model the terms of the Disaster Payment Deferral and complete the settlement
elements.label: text

elements.text: process, Servicers must use the “Payment Deferral” path in Resolve. Additionally, each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer shall comply with the requirements in Section 9203.4(e) to complete the
elements.label: text

elements.text: submission and settlement process for a Disaster Payment Deferral.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (xii) Reporting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: In most cases, the Disaster Payment Deferral does not have an associated unique EDR
elements.label: text

elements.text: status code. For each Mortgage subject to the Disaster Payment Deferral, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must continue reporting the appropriate delinquency status through the Loan Level
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reporting tool in accordance with requirements in Section 8303.3(g) and default
elements.label: text

elements.text: information to Freddie Mac through EDR in accordance with requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9102.6. Once the Disaster Payment Deferral has been completed and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is brought current, the Servicer must report the Mortgage as current through the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Level Reporting tool.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Resolve automatically reports Status Code H6, Payment Deferral Offer, to EDR on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: third Business Day of the following month in which the event occurred. Therefore, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer is not required to report Status Code H6 in connection with a Payment Deferral.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, the Servicer is not prohibited from reporting and may report Status Code H6,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment Deferral Offer, to notify Freddie Mac that the Mortgage is subject to an active
elements.label: text

elements.text: Disaster Payment Deferral offer in the following instances:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The forbearance period ends prior to settlement of an accepted Disaster Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Deferral (e.g., the Servicer elected to use a processing month and the forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: expires), or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer has made a proactive offer following the expiration of a forbearance
elements.label: text

elements.text: plan in accordance with the “Solicitation for a Disaster Payment Deferral” section
elements.label: text

elements.text: above
elements.label: text

elements.text: In these instances, if the Servicer has elected to report Status Code H6, it should continue
elements.label: text

elements.text: to report the code until the offer has expired or the Payment Deferral has been completed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (xiii) Other requirements for the Disaster Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Delinquency status reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Disaster Payment Deferral does not have an associated unique EDR status code.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each Mortgage subject to the Disaster Payment Deferral, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: continue reporting the appropriate delinquency status information to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: through the EDR tool in accordance with requirements in Section 9102.6 and Exhibit
elements.label: text

elements.text: 88. After the Disaster Payment Deferral has been completed and the Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: brought current, the EDR status code must reflect the Mortgage as current.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Borrower redefaults and enters a new forbearance plan, the Servicer must report
elements.label: text

elements.text: the new plan through the EDR tool in accordance with the requirements described
elements.label: text

elements.text: above.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: (C)Credit reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each Mortgage that is subject to the Disaster Payment Deferral, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide a “full file” status report describing the status of the Mortgage to each of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: four major credit repositories in accordance with the credit bureau standards as
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided by the Consumer Data Industry Association.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (D)Incentive payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer is eligible to receive a $500 incentive payment for each completed
elements.label: text

elements.text: Disaster Payment Deferral.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (E) Servicing fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer will continue to receive the Servicing fee it was receiving prior to
elements.label: text

elements.text: completing the Disaster Payment Deferral.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (F) Future Freddie Mac Flex Modification evaluations
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer is evaluating a Borrower for a future Freddie Mac Flex Modification,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Disaster Payment Deferral will not count as a previous loan modification for
elements.label: text

elements.text: purposes of calculating the number of times the Mortgage has previously been
elements.label: text

elements.text: modified.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (xiv) Future Payment Deferral evaluations
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer is evaluating a Borrower for a future non-Disaster Payment Deferral in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements of Sections 9203.4(b) through 9203.4(h), the Disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment Deferral will not cause the Borrower to be ineligible.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (xv) Additional requirements for Mortgages with buydown funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: The additional requirements for Mortgages with buydown funds are as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When processing a Disaster Payment Deferral for Mortgages subject to a temporary
elements.label: text

elements.text: subsidy buydown plan in accordance with Section 4204.3, the Servicer must not
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements of a Disaster Payment Deferral as specified in
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When evaluating a Borrower with a Mortgage with a temporary subsidy buydown
elements.label: text

elements.text: plan for a Payment Deferral, the Servicer must confirm the Borrower’s financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: capacity to continue making the existing contractual monthly Mortgage payment,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including amounts due as a result of an Escrow shortage, as specified in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Upon completion of the Disaster Payment Deferral, the Borrower must resume their
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment amount as specified in the terms of the buydown agreement, subject to
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law and the terms of the buydown agreement*
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Buydown term has expired or if, for any reason, the buydown funds are not
elements.label: text

elements.text: available or the buydown funds are not paid, the Borrower is responsible to continue
elements.label: text

elements.text: to make full monthly Mortgage payments as required by the terms of the Note
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: buydown term or the liquidation of the Mortgage, whichever occurs first, the Servicer

elements.text: must distribute in accordance with applicable law and per the buydown agreement or
elements.label: text

elements.text: apply to the Mortgage consistent with Section 4204.3(e)
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: the terms of the buydown agreement, applicable law and the Guide.

elements.text: Chapter 9204: Freddie Mac Workout Options
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 9204.1: Freddie Mac workout options and Servicer
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: responsibilities (11/19/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac workout options
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer approval authority for workouts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac’s rights related to workout options
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Freddie Mac workout options
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must evaluate Borrowers in accordance with the Freddie Mac loss mitigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluation hierarchy set forth in Section 9201.2. The evaluation hierarchy states the order in
elements.label: text

elements.text: which a Servicer is to evaluate a Borrower for Freddie Mac’s available workout options
elements.label: text

elements.text: which include Mortgage modifications, workout Mortgage assumptions, short sales, deeds￾in-lieu of foreclosure and charge-offs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Borrower’s situation does not meet all the requirements for a particular workout option
elements.label: text

elements.text: but the Servicer believes that the workout option is still the best solution to the Delinquency,
elements.label: text

elements.text: then the Servicer may submit a recommendation and rationale for the recommendation to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac for review, in accordance with the submission procedures in this chapter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Servicer approval authority for workouts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Servicers are delegated to approve the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Freddie Mac Flex Modification®, which is a modification that meets the requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Sections 9206.1(b) through 9206.4(c) in accordance with the requirements of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide or other Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Sections 1301.2(h) and 9206.1(c) for additional information about
elements.label: text

elements.text: delegated authority and adverse action notice requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Freddie Mac Standard Short Sale (“short sale”), including the Streamlined Short Sale,
elements.label: text

elements.text: that meets the requirements of Sections 9208.1(a) through 9208.3(a). All other short sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be submitted to Freddie Mac for review and approval.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Freddie Mac Standard Deed-in-Lieu of Foreclosure (“deed-in-lieu of foreclosure”),
elements.label: text

elements.text: including the Streamlined Deed-in-Lieu of Foreclosure, that meets the requirements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 9209.1 through 9209.6. All other deeds-in-lieu of foreclosure must be submitted
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Freddie Mac for review and approval.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all other workouts in the Guide, the Servicer must submit its recommendation for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: workout to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) for review. Additionally, if the Mortgage is an
elements.label: text

elements.text: FHA, VA or RHS Mortgage, the Servicer must obtain approval from the FHA, RHS, or VA,
elements.label: text

elements.text: if applicable. The Servicer must also obtain any necessary approvals from the MI for any
elements.label: text

elements.text: workout on a Mortgage with mortgage insurance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Freddie Mac’s rights related to workout options
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s approval or settlement of a workout does not limit its right to review the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file and invoke its remedies under the Guide. If Freddie Mac’s review of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file discloses any failure to comply with the Guide or any other Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents, Freddie Mac has the right to require the Servicer to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Compensate Freddie Mac and hold it harmless for any loss, damage or expense
elements.label: text

elements.text: (including court costs, attorney fees and incentive payments) that Freddie Mac sustains
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Repurchase Freddie Mac’s interest in the Mortgage at any time under any of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances outlined in Sections 3602.2(a) and 3602.2(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9204.2: Processing and documentation for workout
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: recommendations (10/08/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Steps for processing a workout recommendation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower documentation for workout options
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Steps for processing a workout recommendation
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Steps for completing a workout
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following are the required steps for completing a workout:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Servicer must collect and analyze the required documentation and information in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with this chapter and Chapter 9202
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. If the Servicer does not have delegated authority to approve the workout, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must submit the required documentation and its recommendation to Freddie Mac for
elements.label: text

elements.text: review through Resolve®. Freddie Mac will review the documentation and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s recommendation and advise the Servicer of its decision. The Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: ensure that all approval conditions are met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. If the Servicer does have delegated authority to approve the workout, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must evaluate the Borrower and process the workout in accordance with the Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: and other Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The Servicer must perform all activities required for closing a workout within Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s required time frames
elements.label: text

elements.text: Closing a workout is the process to complete a workout transaction. Closing includes a
elements.label: text

elements.text: settlement transaction (if applicable), recording any required documents with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate government jurisdiction, timely and accurately reporting the transaction to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, remitting all funds collected as part of the workout to Freddie Mac and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ensuring that Freddie Mac receives the Servicer’s request for reimbursement of any
elements.label: text

elements.text: allowable expenses within 30 days of the settlement date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the steps described above, the Servicer must comply with the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements when submitting a workout solution via Resolve for settlement, regardless
elements.label: text

elements.text: of whether the workout was approved under the Servicer’s delegated authority or
elements.label: text

elements.text: approved by Freddie Mac through the exception process.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Borrower documentation for workout options
elements.label: text

elements.text: Generally, all Borrowers must submit a complete Borrower Response Package in order to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered for a workout option under this chapter unless otherwise specified. Section 9102.5
elements.label: text

elements.text: describes the contents of a Borrower Response Package.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must have:

elements.text: 2. Notified Freddie Mac that the Mortgage is current or is paid in full

elements.text: (b) Prohibition of certain Borrower fees; non-refusal of workout options due to late charges
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may not charge any additional fees to the Borrower other than those provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: for in Freddie Mac’s relief and workout options. The Servicer may not refuse to consider a
elements.label: text

elements.text: workout option or require payment of accrued late charges as a condition of doing a workout.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Mortgage insurance claims
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will file a claim for loss with the MI if the Mortgage is covered by mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance, and Freddie Mac will manage the claims payment process with the MI. The
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: If the MI reduces, suspends or denies the claim due to the Servicer’s actions or inactions,

elements.text: Chapter 9205: Home Affordable Modification

elements.text: 9205.1: Transfers of Servicing and document retention
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: requirements (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This chapter provides Servicing requirements with respect to those Borrowers who received a
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification under the Home Affordable Modification ProgramSM (HAMP®). Servicers may no
elements.label: text

elements.text: longer evaluate Borrowers for a HAMP Trial Period Plan or enter into a HAMP modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement with a Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Document retention requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Transfer of Servicing includes Mortgages modified under HAMP, the Transferor
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must provide special notification to the Transferee Servicer. This includes:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Informing the Transferee Servicer that Mortgages modified under HAMP are part of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: portfolio being transferred, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Confirming that the Transferee Servicer is aware of and agrees to assume the additional
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsibilities associated with the Servicing of these Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the portfolio being transferred includes Mortgages modified under HAMP, the Transferor
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Indicate this in the Servicing Transfer Manager tool (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools) as
elements.label: text

elements.text: part of its request for Freddie Mac’s approval for the Subsequent Transfer of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Specify if the transfer includes modified Mortgages that have a step-rate provision, where
elements.label: text

elements.text: the interest rate is subject to incremental increases beginning in year 6 of the modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identify any Mortgages that include a partial principal forbearance
elements.label: text

elements.text: For electronically modified Mortgages, the Transferor Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Indicate that the eModification Agreement is Electronic (as defined in Section 1402.1(a))
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide a list of such Mortgages and indicate the name of the repository holding the
elements.label: text

elements.text: eModification Agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must comply with all other requirements with respect to Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided under Chapter 7101.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Document retention requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must retain all documents and information received during the process of
elements.label: text

elements.text: determining Borrower eligibility for a modification under HAMP, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower Response Package
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Total monthly Mortgage payment and total monthly gross debt payment calculations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Net present value (NPV) calculations and results (Treasury NPV Model and version used,
elements.label: text

elements.text: assumptions, inputs and outputs)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evidence of the Servicer’s application of each step of the waterfall
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Escrow analysis, Escrow advances, and Escrow set up
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must retain all documents and information related to the monthly payments during
elements.label: text

elements.text: and after the Trial Period, as well as incentive payment calculations and such other required
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All documents, records, data and information required to be maintained by the Servicer under
elements.label: text

elements.text: this Chapter 9205 are, will be, and will remain at all times, the property of Freddie Mac. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must retain such documents, records, data and information in a custodial capacity
elements.label: text

elements.text: and otherwise comply with the Mortgage file retention requirements set forth in Chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3302. All documents required to be maintained under this Chapter 9205 must be maintained
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: As it relates to imminent default a Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Document in its servicing system the basis for its determination that a Borrower is in
elements.label: text

elements.text: imminent default; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Retain all documentation and data used to reach its conclusion. The Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation must also include any Imminent Default Indicator® input and output files
elements.label: text

elements.text: and data.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must retain detailed records of Borrower solicitations or Borrower-initiated
elements.label: text

elements.text: inquiries regarding HAMP, the outcome of the evaluation for modification under HAMP and
elements.label: text

elements.text: specific justification with supporting details if the Borrower was determined to be ineligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: for a modification under HAMP.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must maintain appropriate documentary evidence of their HAMP-related activities,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including, but not limited to, the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer’s process for pre-screening non-performing Mortgages against the basic
elements.label: text

elements.text: HAMP requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All HAMP-related communications, whether verbal or written, with or to the Borrower or
elements.label: text

elements.text: person identified in a written authorization by the Borrower and provided to the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: in accordance with Section 9102.5(c) (Authorized Person) (including, but not limited to,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the dates of communications, names of contact person(s), and a summary of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: conversation), including any e-mail correspondence to or from the Borrower or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Authorized Person
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Phone contact with Borrowers or Authorized Persons relating to HAMP
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Policies and procedures that include HAMP-related activities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Training materials relating to HAMP
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pre-screening of Mortgages for HAMP prior to referring any Mortgage to foreclosure or
elements.label: text

elements.text: conducting scheduled foreclosure sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Postponement of scheduled foreclosure sales in applicable scenarios
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Substitution of income documents for Borrowers in active Chapter 7 or Chapter 13
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Certification prior to foreclosure sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evidence of receipt of the Borrower Response Package from a Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any reports, memoranda, or other documentation relating to HAMP
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The decision-making process when applying good business judgment in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: HAMP and, where applicable, referencing the Servicer’s associated policies and
elements.label: text

elements.text: procedures
elements.label: text

elements.text: With respect to phone contact with Borrowers or Authorized Persons related to HAMP, well￾documented Servicer system notes (including, but not limited to date, names of contact
elements.label: text

elements.text: persons, and a summary of the conversation) constitute appropriate documentation. Written
elements.label: text

elements.text: correspondence should be retained in the Mortgage file and made available upon request by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Records must also be retained to document the reason(s) for a Borrower’s failure to
elements.label: text

elements.text: successfully complete the Trial Period, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When a HAMP modification is not pursued because the NPV test result is negative. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must document its consideration of other alternatives to foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When a Borrower under a HAMP modification loses good standing by becoming 90 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: or more past due (e.g., three monthly payments are due and unpaid on the last day of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: third month). The Servicer must retain documentation of its consideration of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower for other loss mitigation alternatives.
elements.label: text

elements.text: With respect to requirements related to the request for Borrower and co-Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: information for government monitoring purposes, the Servicer must retain the appropriate
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must retain required documents for the period set forth in Section 3302.1(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage is subject to an indemnification agreement, the Servicer must remit to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac an annual payment for the amount of all modification-related costs (e.g., interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: rate shortfall) as calculated by Freddie Mac pursuant to Freddie Mac’s “Modification Loss
elements.label: text

elements.text: Amount” methodology. The Modification Loss Amounts due will be calculated on a monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: basis and billed on an annual basis for the life of the modified Mortgage. If the Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject to a partial indemnification, each year the Servicer will be billed the appropriate
elements.label: text

elements.text: percentage of the Modification Loss Amount that corresponds with the partial
elements.label: text

elements.text: indemnification agreement. Modification Loss Amounts will be determined by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: in accordance with a process described in Bulletins 2016-5 and 2017-1.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Pursuant to Section 9204.3(a), the Servicer is not eligible to receive an incentive for
elements.label: text

elements.text: completing a modification on a Mortgage that is subject to an indemnification agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must comply with the requirements of Section 8303.3(k) in the event of an
elements.label: text

elements.text: understatement or overstatement of principal reduction to either the interest-bearing UPB or
elements.label: text

elements.text: the deferred UPB.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event of an overpayment of disbursed funds to the Servicer, a correction of such
elements.label: text

elements.text: overpayment will result in a draft back of any funds owed to Freddie Mac. If a previously
elements.label: text

elements.text: applied BIC payment must be reduced or reversed, the UPB of the Mortgage will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: increased in the same manner in which the BIC payment was previously applied. That is, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: the BIC payment was applied as a reduction to the interest-bearing UPB, then the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must report an increase in the interest-bearing UPB for the amount of the correction, using

elements.text: the principal balance correction exception code.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Exhibit 60 for information on reporting a principal balance correction
elements.label: text

elements.text: exception code.
