elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Electronic Home Affordable Modification ProgramSM (HAMP®) documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ HAMP eModification agreements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers should be familiar with the following definitions used in this section as defined in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 1401 for:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Defined terms used in this section
elements.label: text

elements.text: E
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Relating to technology having electrical, digital, magnetic, wireless,
elements.label: text

elements.text: optical, electromagnetic, or similar capabilities, as defined in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: “UETA” and/or “E-SIGN”
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Record A “Record” created, generated, sent, communicated, received, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: stored by “Electronic” means, as defined in the “UETA” and/or “E￾SIGN.” The term also includes a paper document converted into an
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Record.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: Signature
elements.label: text

elements.text: An “Electronic” sound, symbol or process attached to, or logically
elements.label: text

elements.text: associated with, a contract or other “Record” and executed or
elements.label: text

elements.text: adopted by a person with the intent to sign the “Record,” as defined
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the “UETA” and/or “E-SIGN”
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: An action or set of actions occurring between two or more persons
elements.label: text

elements.text: relating to the conduct of business, commercial, or governmental
elements.label: text

elements.text: affairs, using “Electronic” means, as defined in the “UETA” and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: “E-SIGN”
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Electronic HAMP documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: In lieu of having the Servicer or Borrower, as applicable, prepare, sign and return paper
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents, certain documents may be prepared, signed and sent electronically by a Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Borrower or by a Borrower to the Servicer, provided such Electronic Transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: comply with the requirements of Chapter 1401 and this section. With the exception of IRS
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 4506T-EZ, Short Form Request for Individual Tax Return Transcript, or IRS Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4506-T, Request for Transcript of Tax Return, all documentation required to be prepared,
elements.label: text

elements.text: signed or sent by either the Servicer or the Borrower under HAMP are considered “loss
elements.label: text

elements.text: mitigation documents” for purposes of Section 1401.3(h).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 9205.3(c) for additional requirements related to the Home Affordable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification Agreement (the HAMP eModification Agreement).
elements.label: text

elements.text: All Electronic loss mitigation documents, and any other Electronic Mortgage file documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: are Electronic Records, and must be capable of being retrieved and printed in a manner that
elements.label: text

elements.text: accurately reflects the information originally contained in the Electronic Records. All
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Records must be accessible (either electronically or on paper) and promptly made
elements.label: text

elements.text: available to Freddie Mac upon request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) HAMP eModification agreements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Special representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer that allowed Borrowers to receive, transmit or electronically sign a HAMP
elements.label: text

elements.text: eModification Agreement represents and warrants to Freddie Mac that it has complied
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the requirements of the Guide and that the HAMP eModification Agreement is
elements.label: text

elements.text: authentic, its terms are valid and enforceable against the Borrower, and the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintains a First Lien position.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) General requirements applicable to all Freddie Mac HAMP eModification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agreements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Store HAMP eModification Agreements of Freddie Mac-owned Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: electronically under no less stringent requirements than the Servicer follows when
elements.label: text

elements.text: electronically storing its own mortgages in its portfolio
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Consult with their legal counsel to ensure that the Servicer’s use and storage of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: HAMP eModification Agreement complies with all applicable federal, State and local
elements.label: text

elements.text: laws, including, without limitation, the federal Electronic Signatures in Global and
elements.label: text

elements.text: National Commerce (“E-SIGN”) Act and/or the Uniform Electronic Transactions Act
elements.label: text

elements.text: (UETA), as enacted in the local jurisdiction, the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act, and its
elements.label: text

elements.text: implementing regulations, and other applicable privacy, disclosure, and data security
elements.label: text

elements.text: laws and regulations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide for Electronic notarization when required, subject to applicable law
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be able to comply with all Guide requirements to service the Mortgage, as modified
elements.label: text

elements.text: by a HAMP eModification Agreement, including, but not limited to, Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations related to payoff (e.g., cancelation of the Mortgage, Note and HAMP
elements.label: text

elements.text: eModification Agreement), grant of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure, foreclosure,
elements.label: text

elements.text: repurchase of an electronically modified Mortgage, and litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notify Freddie Mac when initiating legal action, including foreclosure, on a Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: that has been electronically modified, and further, must use counsel that has the
elements.label: text

elements.text: experience or demonstrated ability to enforce claims under electronically created
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages, Notes or other financial instruments
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Additional requirements when the HAMP Modification Agreement must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: recorded or in recordable format
elements.label: text

elements.text: If, at the time a Servicer entered into a HAMP Modification Agreement, Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: required the HAMP Modification Agreement to be recorded or in recordable format, a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must record any HAMP eModification Agreement in compliance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: recording jurisdiction’s recordation and electronic format requirements in order to ensure
elements.label: text

elements.text: compliance with the Servicer’s obligations under Section 9206.2(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Document custodial requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: If recordation of a HAMP eModification Agreement that is in recordable format is
elements.label: text

elements.text: required, the Servicer must provide a copy of the recorded HAMP eModification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agreement or a copy of any evidence of recordation together with a copy of the executed
elements.label: text

elements.text: HAMP eModification Agreement to its Document Custodian in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Section 1401.3(e) applicable to recordation of an Electronic Record.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Storage and safekeeping of HAMP eModification Agreements
elements.label: text

elements.text: HAMP eModification Agreements (including printed paper copies of facsimiles of
elements.label: text

elements.text: HAMP eModification Agreements) must be stored in accordance with the Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for storing Mortgage file documents. HAMP eModification Agreements
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be associated with all paper Mortgage file documents so that all Servicing records
elements.label: text

elements.text: (both paper and electronic) are identified with, and associated to, the particular Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi)Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: For requirements related to the Transfers of Servicing of an eModification, refer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9205.1(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vii) Data security requirements and data privacy protection
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must follow the data security requirements in Sections 1302.2 and 1401.2(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the data privacy protection standards in Section 8101.4(d). Servicers are required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintain their Servicing records storage system and conduct periodic information
elements.label: text

elements.text: security reviews of the data stored and maintained in their Servicing records storage
elements.label: text

elements.text: system based on, but not limited to, applicable federal, State and local laws and
elements.label: text

elements.text: regulations and the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to require a Servicer to implement additional security
elements.label: text

elements.text: measures regarding its Servicing records storage system.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (viii) Disaster Recovery/Business Continuity Plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must create and maintain a Disaster Recovery/Business Continuity Plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: (DR/BCP) that includes a backup storage site that is not susceptible to the same types of
elements.label: text

elements.text: major disasters as the primary storage site. The DR/BCP must provide for recovery of
elements.label: text

elements.text: functionality, availability, and data services back to the point of failure within a
elements.label: text

elements.text: commercially reasonable period of time (usually within 48 hours of a disaster).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 9206: Modifications
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 9206.1: Freddie Mac Flex Modification® overview,
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: eligibility and requirements (05/01/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ What is a loan modification?
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When to consider a Freddie Mac Flex Modification®
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligibility and documentation requirements for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ineligibility for Freddie Mac Flex Modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Determining imminent default for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property valuation requirements for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) What is a loan modification?
elements.label: text

elements.text: A modification is a written agreement that the Servicer enters into with the Borrower that
elements.label: text

elements.text: permanently changes one or more of the original terms of the Note, such as:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An increase in the amount of the UPB caused by capitalization of interest or non-interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: arrearages, Escrow amounts and/or other advances
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A change in the Note Rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A change in the monthly payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A change in the maturity date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A forbearance of a portion of the principal balance (no write-off or permanent reduction
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the UPB, delinquent interest or other non-interest arrearages of the Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: allowed)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Change in the product type (e.g., an ARM to a fixed-rate Mortgage)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) When to consider a Freddie Mac Flex Modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must evaluate the Borrower for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification under Chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9206 in accordance with the evaluation hierarchy in Section 9201.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless otherwise notified by Freddie Mac, all Freddie Mac Servicers are delegated to
elements.label: text

elements.text: approve, and must offer, a Freddie Mac Flex Modification to eligible Borrowers in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements of the Guide and other applicable Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 1301.2(i) for additional information about delegated authority and adverse
elements.label: text

elements.text: action notice requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Eligibility and documentation requirements for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be eligible for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification, the Servicer must ensure that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following Borrower and Mortgage eligibility requirements are met and that the Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not otherwise excluded from eligibility as set forth in Section 9206.1(d).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If any of the eligibility requirements are not met, but the Servicer believes, based on an
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluation of a complete Borrower Response Package, that the Borrower should be
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification, the Servicer must transmit the exception
elements.label: text

elements.text: request via Resolve® to Freddie Mac. Refer to Section 9206.2(a) for additional information
elements.label: text

elements.text: on Resolve.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, if there is a Risk of Property Ownership (see Section 9202.2(b)) and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is not otherwise eligible for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification, the Servicer may
elements.label: text

elements.text: submit a recommendation to Freddie Mac to consider a Freddie Mac Flex Modification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event Freddie Mac participated in evaluating a Borrower for a Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification and Freddie Mac denied the request, the Servicer must refer to Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1301.2(i) for more information on adverse action notices that must be provided to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower on behalf of Freddie Mac under certain limited circumstances.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Borrower eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Submit a complete Borrower Response Package and:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Have an eligible hardship as described in Section 9202.1(b). The hardship must
elements.label: text

elements.text: currently be causing or be expected to cause a long-term or permanent decrease in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower’s income or increase in the Borrower’s expenses.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Unemployment is considered a temporary hardship. Servicers must consider
elements.label: text

elements.text: unemployed Borrowers for a forbearance plan under Sections 9203.3(a) through
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Have verified income
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be 60 days or more delinquent, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Is determined to be in imminent default in accordance with Section 9206.1(e)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Mortgage eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must have been originated at least 12 months prior to the evaluation
elements.label: text

elements.text: date for the Freddie Mac Flex Modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must be a conventional First Lien Mortgage currently owned or
elements.label: text

elements.text: guaranteed in whole or in part by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Freddie Mac Flex Modification must result in a principal and interest payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: that is less than or equal to the pre-modification principal and interest payment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (Refer to Section 9206.2(a) for additional payment reduction requirements that may
elements.label: text

elements.text: apply.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: When determining whether the modification results in a principal and interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment that is less than or equal to the pre-modification principal and interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment, the Servicer must consider the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If the Borrower has been granted interest rate relief under the Servicemembers
elements.label: text

elements.text: Civil Relief Act (SCRA), the Servicer must consider the principal and interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment in effect prior to the date the SCRA relief was granted rather than the
elements.label: text

elements.text: temporarily reduced monthly payment based on the SCRA interest rate cap
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If the Mortgage being modified is an ARM or an interest-only Mortgage, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must consider the principal and interest payment or interest-only
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment as applicable, in effect at the time the Servicer determines eligibility for
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Freddie Mac Flex Modification Trial Period Plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Mortgage is subject to an indemnification agreement and is otherwise eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the requirements of this chapter, the Servicer has discretion to approve the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage modification provided the following conditions are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The modified Mortgage retains its credit enhancement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ If the Servicer is not the credit enhancement provider, the Servicer must first
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtain in writing any required approval under the terms of the credit enhancement
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the entity providing the enhancement to enter into a modification agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: that complies with the requirements of this chapter; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Servicer remits to Freddie Mac an annual payment for the amount of all
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification-related costs (e.g., interest rate shortfall) as calculated by Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac pursuant to Freddie Mac’s “Modification Loss Amount” methodology. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification Loss Amounts due will be calculated on a monthly basis and billed
elements.label: text

elements.text: on an annual basis for the life of the modified Mortgage. If the Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject to a partial indemnification, each year, the Servicer will be billed the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate percentage of the Modification Loss Amount that corresponds with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the partial indemnification agreement. Modification Loss Amounts will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: determined by Freddie Mac in accordance with a process described in Bulletins
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Pursuant to Section 9204.3(a), the Servicer is not eligible to receive an
elements.label: text

elements.text: incentive for completing a modification on a Mortgage that is subject to an
elements.label: text

elements.text: indemnification agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Mortgage is secured by a leasehold estate, the term of the lease (or any
elements.label: text

elements.text: exercised option to renew the lease or any renewal options that are enforceable by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: leasehold mortgagee, whichever is applicable) must not terminate earlier than five
elements.label: text

elements.text: years after the maturity date of the proposed modified Mortgage. In the event that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: current term of the lease (or applicable renewal options) terminates earlier than five
elements.label: text

elements.text: years after the maturity date of the proposed modified Mortgage, the term of the lease
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be renegotiated in order to satisfy this requirement prior to offering the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower a Trial Period Plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must refer to the special requirements in Section 9206.1(c)(v) for Borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: who experience a hardship as a result of an Eligible Disaster and who were current or less
elements.label: text

elements.text: than 31 days delinquent as of the date of the disaster.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Streamlined eligibility for certain Borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certain eligibility exceptions apply for a Borrower who:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Is 90 days delinquent or greater as of the evaluation date; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Has a Step-Rate Mortgage and:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Becomes 60 days delinquent within the 12 months following the first payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: due date resulting from an interest rate adjustment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Has not submitted a complete Borrower Response Package
elements.label: text

elements.text: For these Borrowers, the eligibility requirements in Section 9206.1(c)(i) are not
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable. In these instances, a Borrower Response Package is not required, and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer is not required to confirm a Borrower’s hardship or income. The Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: continue to comply with the requirements in Sections 9206.1(c)(ii) and 9206.1(d) to
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine eligibility.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Payments received after the evaluation date do not impact Borrower eligibility even
elements.label: text

elements.text: if the payments result in the Mortgage becoming less than 90 days delinquent.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must evaluate the Borrower for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification Trial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Period Plan offer in accordance with the eligibility requirements described in this section
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the solicitation requirements in Section 9102.5 to an eligible Borrower who:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Becomes 90 days delinquent, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Has a Step-Rate Mortgage and becomes 60 days delinquent within the 12 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: following the first payment due date resulting from an interest rate adjustment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Before offering such Borrower a Freddie Mac Flex Modification Trial Period Plan,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must either obtain the applicable MI’s approval of the terms of each
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification on a case-by-case basis or ensure that the applicable MI has provided a
elements.label: text

elements.text: delegation of authority that applies to the requested modification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Payments received after the evaluation date do not impact Borrower eligibility even
elements.label: text

elements.text: if the payments result in the Mortgage becoming less than 90 days delinquent.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 9102.5 for additional information on solicitation of delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Complete Borrower Response Package received
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower submits a complete Borrower Response Package prior to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: becoming 90 days delinquent, the Servicer must acknowledge receipt of the package and
elements.label: text

elements.text: review it in accordance with the evaluation hierarchy found in Section 9201.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must send an eligible Borrower an offer for a Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification in accordance with the requirements of Section 9206.1(c)(iii) once the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower reaches the applicable Delinquency threshold if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer has not received a complete Borrower Response Package, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer previously conducted an evaluation of a complete Borrower Response
elements.label: text

elements.text: Package and determined that the Borrower was not eligible for any alternative to
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower has rejected all other alternatives to foreclosure offered by the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, if a Borrower with a Step-Rate Mortgage submits a complete Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: Response Package prior to becoming 90 days delinquent, and the Borrower has not yet
elements.label: text

elements.text: accepted the offer for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification, the Servicer must complete its
elements.label: text

elements.text: review of the package for all alternatives to foreclosure in accordance with the Guide. If
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower is eligible for additional payment relief, then the Servicer must permit the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower to continue making the existing Trial Period Plan payments but must update the
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification agreement to reflect the lower payment amount. The post-modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: principal and interest payment must reflect the lower payment amount in these instances.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Special requirements for Borrowers impacted by an Eligible Disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: The requirements of this Section 9206.1(c)(v) apply to Borrowers who were current or
elements.label: text

elements.text: less than 60 days delinquent as of the date the Eligible Disaster occurred, experienced a
elements.label: text

elements.text: hardship as a result of the Eligible Disaster and were at least 90 days delinquent as of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluation date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Payments received after the evaluation date do not impact eligibility even if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment results in the Mortgage becoming less than 90 days delinquent.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following special requirements apply when the Freddie Mac Flex Modification is
elements.label: text

elements.text: offered as a result of the Borrower experiencing a hardship as the result of an Eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: Disaster:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer is not required to have previously solicited the Borrower for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure prevention alternative
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The following Mortgages are ineligible for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification. These
elements.label: text

elements.text: exclusions are in lieu of the requirements described in Section 9206.1(c)(ii) above
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Section 9206.1(d) below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgage is an FHA, VA or Guaranteed Rural Housing Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgage is subject to recourse
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ With the exception of a disaster-related forbearance plan, the Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: currently performing under another forbearance plan, Trial Period Plan or
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgage is subject to an approved short sale or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgage is currently subject to an unexpired offer to the Borrower for
elements.label: text

elements.text: another modification or other foreclosure prevention alternative, such as a non￾disaster-related forbearance or repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgage is not a conventional First Lien Mortgage currently owned or
elements.label: text

elements.text: guaranteed in whole or in part by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Freddie Mac Flex Modification would result in a principal and interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment that is greater than the pre-modification principal and interest payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgage is secured by a leasehold estate and the term of the lease does not
elements.label: text

elements.text: meet the requirements described in Section 9206.1(c)(ii)
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer was not collecting Escrows on the existing Mortgage, the Borrower is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not required to establish an Escrow account as a condition of the modification unless
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise required by applicable law, or the Servicer confirms that the taxes and
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance premiums have not been paid and are past due.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For special requirements related to solicitations for streamlined offers for Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac Flex Modifications for certain Borrowers, the Servicer must refer to Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9203.4(i). If the Borrower is ineligible for streamlined offers under the requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Section 9203.4(i) and this section but is at least 90 days delinquent, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must evaluate the Borrower in accordance with the requirements of Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Ineligibility for Freddie Mac Flex Modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following Mortgages and Borrowers are ineligible for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ FHA, VA and Guaranteed Rural Housing Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages subject to recourse
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages secured by second homes or non-owner-occupied properties (i.e., Investment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Properties) where the Borrower is current or less than 60 days delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following Mortgages and Borrowers are also ineligible for a Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification. However, if the Servicer believes, based on the Borrower’s individual
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances, that the Borrower should be considered for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer should submit the request to Freddie Mac (refer to Section 9206.1(c) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: information on submitting an exception request to Freddie Mac):
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages that have been previously modified three or more times
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages previously modified with the Freddie Mac Flex Modification terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: determined in accordance with Section 9206.2(a) where:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgage became 60 or more days delinquent within 12 months of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification Effective Date, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Borrower has not brought the Mortgage current following the Delinquency
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrowers who, within 12 months of the evaluation date, failed a Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification Trial Period Plan and the terms of that Trial Period Plan were determined in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 9206.2(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is subject to an approved short sale or deed-in-lieu of foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower is currently performing under another Trial Period Plan, forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: or repayment plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is currently subject to an unexpired offer to the Borrower for another
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification or other alternative to foreclosure, such as a forbearance, repayment plan or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any Borrower who is ineligible for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification must provide a
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete Borrower Response Package in order to be evaluated for the most appropriate
elements.label: text

elements.text: workout solution in accordance with the evaluation hierarchy in Section 9201.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Determining imminent default for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: In order to be eligible for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification, Borrowers who are current or
elements.label: text

elements.text: less than 60 days delinquent must be determined to be in imminent default and must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: occupying the property as a Primary Residence. The Servicer must verify that at least one
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower is occupying the property as a Primary Residence based on a review of a credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: report. If the credit report does not indicate that the property securing the Mortgage is the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Primary Residence for a Borrower, then the Servicer must use good business judgment in
elements.label: text

elements.text: reconciling the inconsistency. This additional due diligence on the part of the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be documented in the Mortgage file/servicing system.
elements.label: text

elements.text: An imminent default evaluation is necessary when the status of the Mortgage is current or
elements.label: text

elements.text: less than 60 days delinquent as of the date the Servicer commences the initial evaluation
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Borrower’s financial condition using the imminent default evaluation business
elements.label: text

elements.text: rules as described in Section 9206.1(e)(ii).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must rely on the same Mortgage status used to initiate the imminent default
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluation to complete the imminent default determination process, regardless of whether
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower becomes 60 days or more delinquent during the imminent default
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Imminent default evaluation business rules
elements.label: text

elements.text: Resolve will evaluate the information the Servicer provides against the imminent default
elements.label: text

elements.text: business rules. Any Borrower who is current or less than 60 days delinquent at the time
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer commences the initial evaluation is in imminent default if the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: meets the requirements of the following business rules:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Imminent Default Evaluation business rules
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be considered in imminent default, the Borrower must meet all requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: under Business rule 1, and must meet the requirements for either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Business rule 2, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Business rule 3
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business rule 1 Each Borrower must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Submit a complete Borrower Response Package
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be current or less than 60 days delinquent as of the evaluation
elements.label: text

elements.text: date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Occupy the property as a Primary Residence; or at least one
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower on the Mortgage must occupy the property as his or her
elements.label: text

elements.text: Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have Cash Reserves less than $25,000
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have an eligible hardship as described in Section 9202.1(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business rule 2 The Borrower is considered in imminent default if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower meets the requirements of business rule 1, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower’s FICO® score is less than or equal to 620
elements.label: text

elements.text: determined in accordance with Section 9206.1(e)(v); AND
elements.label: text

elements.text: EITHER
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgage has had two or more 30-day Delinquencies in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the most recent 6-month period; OR
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Borrower’s pre-modification housing expense-to-income
elements.label: text

elements.text: ratio is greater than 40%
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower has one of the Imminent Default Hardships described
elements.label: text

elements.text: below in business rule 3, the Borrower may be determined to be in
elements.label: text

elements.text: imminent default even if these business rule 2 requirements are not
elements.label: text

elements.text: met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business rule 3 The Borrower is considered in imminent default if the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: meets the requirements of business rule 1, and the Borrower provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: the documentation required in Section 9202.1(b) supporting one of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Imminent Default Hardships listed below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Death of a Borrower or death of either the primary or secondary
elements.label: text

elements.text: wage earner in the household
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Long-term or permanent disability; or serious illness of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower/co-Borrower or dependent family member
elements.label: text

elements.text: Imminent Default Evaluation business rules
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be considered in imminent default, the Borrower must meet all requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: under Business rule 1, and must meet the requirements for either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Business rule 2, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Business rule 3
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Divorce or legal separation; separation of Borrower unrelated by
elements.label: text

elements.text: marriage, civil union or similar domestic partnership under
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Principal and interest payment increase as a result of an interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: adjustment applied to a Step-Rate Mortgage no more than 12
elements.label: text

elements.text: months prior to the evaluation date
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Imminent Default Hardship must currently cause and be
elements.label: text

elements.text: expected to continue to cause a long-term or permanent decrease in
elements.label: text

elements.text: income or increase in expenses.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must enter all information in Resolve for business rule 1 and business rule
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2 in all instances, even if the Borrower does not meet the requirements under business
elements.label: text

elements.text: rule 2 and instead is approved based on the Imminent Default Hardship under business
elements.label: text

elements.text: rule 3.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Income and asset documentation and verification
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A) Documentation and verification
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be evaluated for imminent default, a Borrower must, at a minimum, provide a
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete Borrower Response Package as defined in Section 9102.5(d). In addition to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the income documentation required under Section 9202.1(c), the Servicer must obtain
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower’s FICO score in accordance with Section 9206.1(e)(v).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Verification of income and assets; resolution of material inconsistencies
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must review all documentation submitted by the Borrower to identify any
elements.label: text

elements.text: material inconsistencies, including material inconsistencies with a tax return or tax
elements.label: text

elements.text: transcript if one was obtained under Section 9202.1(c). If, based on the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: good business judgment, there are material inconsistencies with respect to the income
elements.label: text

elements.text: or asset information disclosed by the Borrower or with other documentation relevant
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the imminent default decision, the Servicer must obtain other documentation to
elements.label: text

elements.text: reasonably reconcile such material inconsistencies. Servicers must also document
elements.label: text

elements.text: such material differences in their servicing system. If the Servicer cannot reconcile
elements.label: text

elements.text: such material differences, the Borrower cannot be considered in imminent default.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Cash Reserves test
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must complete an evaluation of the Borrower’s Cash Reserves. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower must have Cash Reserves of less than $25,000 to be further evaluated for
elements.label: text

elements.text: imminent default. If the Borrower either discloses or provides documentation indicating
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower has Cash Reserves equal to or greater than $25,000, then the Borrower is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not in imminent default.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Definition of Cash Reserves
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of determining imminent default, Cash Reserves are defined as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cash Reserves: Any non-retirement liquid asset the Borrower has available for
elements.label: text

elements.text: withdrawal from any financial institution or brokerage, including funds on deposit in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower’s checking, savings, money market or certificate of deposit account or
elements.label: text

elements.text: other depository account, stocks, bonds, mutual funds, U.S. Government Securities
elements.label: text

elements.text: and other securities that are traded on an exchange or marketplace generally available
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the public (e.g., New York Stock Exchange, National Association of Securities
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dealers Automated Quotations, Midwest SE, Chicago Board of Trade or Over the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Counter) for which the price can be readily verified through financial publications.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assets are only considered retirement assets if they are held in a qualified retirement
elements.label: text

elements.text: account such as a 401k 403b, 457, Individual Retirement Account (IRA) or pension
elements.label: text

elements.text: fund. If the assets are not held in a retirement account, the assets must be considered
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cash Reserves.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Calculating Cash Reserves
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must calculate the Borrower’s Cash Reserves in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must determine that, for every Borrower on the Mortgage, all of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s Cash Reserves have been accounted for on Form 710, Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assistance Application
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In making the determination that all Cash Reserves have been accounted for, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must review all information provided by the Borrower to determine if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: asset information stated on Form 710 is reasonably consistent with information
elements.label: text

elements.text: available from all other information provided by the Borrower, including verbal
elements.label: text

elements.text: information shared by the Borrower. If there are inconsistencies between the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s disclosure of assets and the information provided by the Borrower,
elements.label: text

elements.text: then the Servicer must obtain the Borrower’s tax return or tax transcript in order
elements.label: text

elements.text: to reconcile the inconsistencies.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If, upon reviewing the Borrower’s tax return or tax transcript, if applicable, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer observes interest, dividend income or gains/losses that, in total, that
elements.label: text

elements.text: could not be reasonably produced by the Borrower’s disclosed Cash Reserves,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and such income indicates deposits, securities holdings or other assets that could
elements.label: text

elements.text: be in excess of the amounts disclosed by the Borrower on Form 710, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must reconcile the inconsistency with the Borrower. The Servicer must require the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower to produce a signed federal tax return and all relevant schedules, in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: event the Servicer used a tax transcript in lieu of a tax return, along with any other
elements.label: text

elements.text: relevant documentation that verifies the disposition and/or current status of those
elements.label: text

elements.text: assets, which produced the income or gains/losses to resolve the inconsistency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must ensure that the Borrower’s disclosure of assets is reasonably
elements.label: text

elements.text: accurate despite the inconsistency between the disclosed assets and the income or
elements.label: text

elements.text: gain/loss from assets reported on the tax return or tax transcript. In determining
elements.label: text

elements.text: what documentation is needed to reconcile an inconsistency, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: review the detailed tax return schedules and forms, and request from the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: copies of recent and past statements from those asset holdings or transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: indicated on the schedules and forms that produced the income or gain/loss (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: checking, savings, brokerage account statements, asset sale statements or records,
elements.label: text

elements.text: etc.).
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If there are inconsistencies between the Borrower’s disclosure of assets and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: tax return information that cannot be reconciled, the Borrower cannot be
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered in imminent default
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer determines that the Borrower has Cash Reserves of less than $25,000
elements.label: text

elements.text: and meets all other requirements of Section 9206.1(e)(ii) then the Borrower is
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered to be in imminent default.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Imminent default Credit Score
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must choose one FICO score that is adequately indicative of the credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: reputation of all Borrowers currently on the Mortgage. Servicers must use a Credit Score
elements.label: text

elements.text: based on the FICO credit-scoring model. This score must be obtained and determined in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Obtaining FICO scores for each Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: (I) Borrowers with FICO scores
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must request a FICO score for each Borrower on the Mortgage from
elements.label: text

elements.text: any one of the following three credit repositories:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Equifax Credit Information Services
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Experian Information Systems and Services
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ TransUnion Credit Information Company
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower’s FICO score must be less than 90 days old on the date the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: performs the imminent default evaluation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (II)Borrowers with no available FICO score
elements.label: text

elements.text: It is unusual for any Borrower who has obtained a Mortgage not to have a FICO
elements.label: text

elements.text: score. If no single FICO score can be identified for a Borrower, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: recheck the information provided when ordering the FICO scores and resubmit a
elements.label: text

elements.text: request. If the Servicer is still unable to obtain a FICO score for that Borrower, it
elements.label: text

elements.text: may rely on the FICO scores of all other Borrowers as determined in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Absent a FICO score for any Borrower on the Mortgage, the Borrower may not be
elements.label: text

elements.text: determined to be in imminent default under the requirements of business rule 2,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the Servicer must proceed to evaluate the Borrower under the requirements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: business rule 3 in Section 9206.1(e)(ii). In such instances when a FICO score is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not available for any Borrower on the Mortgage, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Maintain documentation in the Mortgage file that demonstrates the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: attempts to obtain FICO scores from all three credit repositories on all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Enter the result that a FICO score is not available for any Borrower on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage into Resolve
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Proceed to the Imminent Default Hardship test in Business Rule 3 to
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine if an Imminent Default Hardship exists
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Determining the Imminent Default Credit Score
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must identify the Imminent Default Credit Score in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must first select a single FICO score for each Borrower on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage. If the Servicer obtains multiple FICO scores for a single Borrower, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must use the middle/lower method to select the single FICO score for
elements.label: text

elements.text: that Borrower. This method is the most predictive when determining a single
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s overall credit reputation. If three FICO scores are obtained for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower, the single score for that Borrower is the one with the middle value. For
elements.label: text

elements.text: example, if the FICO scores were 660, 656 and 640, the single FICO score
elements.label: text

elements.text: selected by the Servicer should be 656. When there is a duplicate score, the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: must select that score to be the single score. If the FICO scores for a Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: were 660, 660 and 640, the Servicer should select 660. If two FICO scores were
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtained for a Borrower, the Servicer must select the lower of the two FICO
elements.label: text

elements.text: scores to be the single FICO score for that Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If there is only one Borrower on the Mortgage, the single FICO score, determined
elements.label: text

elements.text: in accordance with the above requirements, is considered the Imminent Default
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit Score
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If there are multiple Borrowers on the Mortgage, the Servicer must determine the
elements.label: text

elements.text: single FICO score for each Borrower using the method described above. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must then select either the lowest FICO score across all Borrowers on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage or the average FICO score from all Borrowers’ single scores. (Note:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Whichever method is used, the Servicer should choose the single FICO score
elements.label: text

elements.text: using the same method and procedure for all Borrowers and for all Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: consistent with fair lending laws.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi)Calculating pre-modification housing expense-to-income ratio
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must input verified income data into Resolve. Based on the Servicer’s input,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Resolve will determine if the Borrower’s pre-modification housing expense-to-income
elements.label: text

elements.text: ratio is greater than 40%. For purposes of this determination, the Borrower’s current
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly housing expense is divided by the Borrower’s monthly gross income (or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers’ combined monthly gross income in the case of co-Borrowers) plus any
elements.label: text

elements.text: allowable non-obligor household income.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Borrower’s current monthly housing expense consists of the following, as applicable:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monthly principal and interest payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monthly pro rata amount for real estate taxes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monthly pro rata amount for property or flood insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monthly pro rata amount for homeowners association (HOA) dues, Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unit or Cooperative Unit Maintenance Fees and ground rent
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any escrow shortage currently included as part of the monthly contractual payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower has been granted interest rate relief under the Servicemembers Civil
elements.label: text

elements.text: Relief Act (SCRA), the Servicer must use the principal and interest payment and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: contractual rate of interest in effect on the Note prior to the granting of the SCRA relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: rather than the temporarily SCRA reduced interest rate and related SCRA monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment when calculating the Borrower’s current monthly housing expense-to-income
elements.label: text

elements.text: ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Borrower has indicated that there are condominium/HOA or Cooperative Corporation
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessments (see Chapter 8801 for special Servicing requirements for Cooperative Share
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loans), Condominium Unit maintenance fees or Cooperative Unit Maintenance Fees, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ground rents, but has not been able to provide written documentation to verify these
elements.label: text

elements.text: amounts, the Servicer must rely on the information provided by the Borrower if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer has made reasonable efforts to obtain the amounts in writing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The current monthly housing expense does not include mortgage insurance premium
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments, payments due to holders of subordinate liens, or projected escrow shortages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Based on the information provided by the Servicer, Resolve will calculate the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: housing-to-expense income ratio and determine if it meets the imminent default
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements under Business Rule 2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vii)Imminent default evaluation results
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Resolve determines the Borrower meets the requirements of business rule 1 and meets
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirements of either: (i) business rule 2, or (ii) business rule 3, the Borrower is in
elements.label: text

elements.text: imminent default. The Servicer must evaluate the Borrower for a Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification Trial Period Plan and no further analysis is required by the Servicer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine imminent default.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (viii) General requirements and information
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Resolve determines that a Borrower is in imminent default, the Servicer must continue
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluating the Borrower using the applicable underwriting requirements outlined in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 9206 to determine if the Borrower qualifies for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Property valuation requirements for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Based on the information provided by the Servicer, Resolve will determine the property value
elements.label: text

elements.text: of each Mortgage under consideration for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9206.2: Modification terms, trial period and conditions
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: of a Freddie Mac Flex Modification® (04/08/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Determining the terms of a Freddie Mac Flex Modification®
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Trial Period Plan requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Modified Mortgage conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other modification conditions and requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Determining the terms of a Freddie Mac Flex Modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Based on the information provided by the Servicer, Resolve® will determine the terms of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Flex Modification Trial Period Plan and, following the Trial Period Plan, will
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine the terms of the final modification agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the Borrower has previously made partial prepayments of principal (curtailments)
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the monthly principal and interest installments were not recalculated in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 8103.3(d), then the remaining Mortgage term must be calculated using the pre￾modification interest-bearing UPB and the pre-modification principal and interest payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The payment reduction target is considered satisfied once:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An incremental application of the steps to determine the terms of a Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification (i.e., interest rate application, Mortgage term extension, principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance) achieves a post-modified Principal and Interest Payment reduction amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: that just surpasses but is as close to 20% as possible (e.g., 20.01%)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The payment reduction target is not considered satisfied if the payment reduction amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: is less than or precisely equal to 20%
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must apply the steps to determine the terms for the Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification incrementally until the payment reduction target is achieved or until the steps
elements.label: text

elements.text: are exhausted. The Servicer must offer the Freddie Mac Flex Modification if the modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: will result in a post-modified Principal and Interest Payment amount that is less than or equal
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the pre-modified payment amount. Based on the information provided by the Servicer,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Resolve will determine the terms of the Freddie Mac Flex Modification according to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following steps:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Determining the terms of a Freddie Mac Flex Modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Steps Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Step 1 Capitalize arrearages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Capitalize arrearages in accordance with the requirements of Section 9206.3(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Step 2 Establish the preliminary modification interest rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Set the preliminary modification interest rate to a fixed rate based on the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements using the interest rate in effect for the periodic payment due in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: month of the evaluation date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: This step is solely to set the preliminary modification interest rate. Refer
elements.label: text

elements.text: to step 3 for instructions on how to apply interest rate relief pursuant to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Plan offer within 14 days of the date of the offer constitutes sufficient notice solely
elements.label: text

elements.text: for purposes of suspending foreclosure referral or sale in accordance with Sections
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of legal acceptance, Borrowers are not required to sign or return the
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Period Plan Notice is evidence of the Borrower’s acceptance of the Trial Period Plan

elements.text: terms and conditions. The Servicer must receive the Borrower’s first Trial Period
