elements.text: (ii) Requirements during the Trial Period
elements.label: text

elements.text: The first Trial Period payment is due by the Trial Period Plan Offer Deadline. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must require the Borrower to remit timely payments. Each Trial Period payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be received no later than the last day of the month in which the Trial Period
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment is due. Borrowers who fail to make timely Trial Period payments are considered
elements.label: text

elements.text: to have failed the Trial Period. Servicers must use good business judgment in determining
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether Trial Period payments were received timely or if mitigating circumstances
elements.label: text

elements.text: caused the payment to be late. Exceptions must be documented in the Servicer’s records.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Although the Borrower may make scheduled Trial Period payments earlier than expected,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the payments may not result in acceleration of the Modification Effective Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: During the Trial Period, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Continue to report to Freddie Mac in accordance with the investor reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements set forth in the Guide, which include the advancing of forecasted
elements.label: text

elements.text: scheduled interest (and principal, if applicable) under the existing Mortgage terms to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, provided that the Servicer has not inactivated the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credit to an unapplied or suspense funds account, payments made by the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: during the Trial Period. Once enough funds have accumulated in the unapplied or
elements.label: text

elements.text: suspense funds account to satisfy the oldest payment due under the existing Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: terms (including applying the portion of the Trial Period payment allocable to
elements.label: text

elements.text: escrowed items to the existing or newly established Escrow account provided those
elements.label: text

elements.text: amounts were due at the time of the oldest delinquent payment due date), the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must apply the payment in accordance with the current Note and Security Instrument,
elements.label: text

elements.text: or prior modification agreement, if applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Recommencement and/or initiation of collection efforts and foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: actions
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Borrower is considered to have failed a Trial Period Plan if the Borrower fails
elements.label: text

elements.text: to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Make a Trial Period payment by the last day of the month in which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment is due
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Comply with the terms of the Trial Period Plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower fails the Trial Period Plan because they did not meet the above
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements, the Servicer must begin or recommence collection efforts in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 9102.4 or, if applicable, recommence any suspended
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure action or proceeding.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, the Servicer must cancel the plan if the Borrower does not execute
elements.label: text

elements.text: and return the modification agreement per the requirements set forth in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9206.4(a), the Servicer must begin or recommence collection efforts in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 9102.4 or, if applicable, recommence any suspended
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure action or proceeding.
elements.label: text

elements.text: After determining a Borrower has failed or the Trial Period was canceled
elements.label: text

elements.text: according to the requirements under this Section 9206.2(b)(ii), the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: report any initiated or resumed collection or foreclosure activity through EDR.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 9102.6 for information on EDR.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Late charges may accrue during the Trial Period subject to the requirements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9102.2. However, all accrued and unpaid late charges must be waived in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the event the Mortgage is modified.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Changes to tax and insurance premium payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: If there are changes in a Borrower’s tax and insurance premium payments after
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower has been qualified for a Trial Period Plan, the Servicer is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: required to re-qualify the Borrower based on the subsequent changes in taxes and
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance. However, the Servicer should provide written notice to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: that explains the impact of the new Escrow payment on the modification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Borrowers in Trial Period Plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Borrower was in a Trial Period Plan prior to entering into a forbearance plan,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower may be re-evaluated for a new Trial Period Plan within 30 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior to or upon completion of the forbearance plan. Servicers must not resume or
elements.label: text

elements.text: restart the terms of the previous Trial Period Plan prior to the start of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan. Instead, the Servicer must evaluate the Borrower based on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: status of the Mortgage at the time of the new evaluation and, if the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: meets all eligibility requirements, the Servicer must send a new Trial Period Plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: offer to commence on or after the completion of the forbearance plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For any subsequent modification submissions, the Trial Period Plan that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower was in prior to the start of the forbearance plan will not be considered a
elements.label: text

elements.text: failed Trial Period Plan for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification evaluation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Borrowers filing for bankruptcy during the Trial Period
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers who are in a Trial Period Plan and subsequently file for bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: may not be denied a modification on the basis of the bankruptcy filing. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer and its counsel must work with the Borrower or Borrower’s counsel to
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtain any court and/or trustee approvals required in accordance with local court
elements.label: text

elements.text: rules and procedures. Servicers should extend the Trial Period Plan as necessary
elements.label: text

elements.text: to accommodate delays in obtaining court approvals or receiving a full remittance
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Borrower’s Trial Period payments when they are made to a trustee, but they
elements.label: text

elements.text: must not extend the Trial Period beyond nine months, resulting in a total 12-
elements.label: text

elements.text: month Trial Period. In the event of a Trial Period extension, the Borrower must
elements.label: text

elements.text: make a Trial Period payment for each month of the Trial Period, including any
elements.label: text

elements.text: extension month, in order to remain eligible for a modification. See Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9206.2(d)(i) for information on reporting an extended Trial Period Plan via EDR.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Chapter 13 bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Borrower in an active Chapter 13 bankruptcy is in a Trial Period Plan and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower has made post-petition payments on the Mortgage in the amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: required by the Trial Period Plan, a Servicer must not object to confirmation of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s Chapter 13 plan, move for relief from the automatic bankruptcy stay
elements.label: text

elements.text: or move for dismissal of the Chapter 13 case on the basis that the Borrower paid
elements.label: text

elements.text: only the amounts due under the Trial Period Plan, as opposed to the non-modified
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage payments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Chapter 7 bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers who have received a Chapter 7 bankruptcy discharge in a case
elements.label: text

elements.text: involving the Mortgage and who did not reaffirm the Mortgage debt under
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law, are eligible for a modification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Mortgages with temporary subsidy buydown plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: When processing the Trial Period Plan offer on a Mortgage with a temporary
elements.label: text

elements.text: subsidy buydown plan, the Servicer must make the appropriate changes to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable evaluation notice to reflect application of any buydown funds as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: condition of completing the Mortgage modification, as applicable. See Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9206.3(b)(i) for information about temporary subsidy buydown funds.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Modified Mortgage conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must ensure that the modified Mortgage:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Retains its First Lien position and continues to be fully enforceable in accordance with its
elements.label: text

elements.text: terms at the time of modification, throughout its modified term, and during any
elements.label: text

elements.text: bankruptcy or foreclosure proceeding involving the Mortgage. The Servicer must record
elements.label: text

elements.text: the modification agreement only when doing so is necessary to ensure its compliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with this First Lien retention and modification enforcement requirement. If recordation is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not immediately necessary but may be required in the future to comply with this First
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lien retention and modification enforcement requirement, the Servicer must have the
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification agreement in recordable form. The modification agreement must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: executed by the Borrower(s).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Ensure the following are current, as applicable: property taxes ground rents and
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessments or other charges that, if delinquent, are or may become First Liens on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property or that if not paid would result in the subordination of Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: interests. (See Section 9301.6(e) regarding expenses that may become First Liens on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the property.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Obtain a title endorsement or similar title insurance product issued by a title insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: company prior to or at the time of the modification whenever it is necessary to record
elements.label: text

elements.text: the modification agreement to retain the modified Mortgage’s First Lien position. For
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Share Loans recognized as personal property, refer to Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8801.1(f)(i) regarding certain Servicer warranties required in the event of a loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Obtain subordination agreements from any junior lienholders, if required by the title
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance company.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Record the executed loan modification agreement, even if the jurisdiction where the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property is located does not require the Servicer to do so, whenever recordation is
elements.label: text

elements.text: necessary to retain the modified Mortgage’s First Lien position if, in the future,
elements.label: text

elements.text: recordation is necessary to enforce the terms of the modified Mortgage (e.g., pre￾foreclosure) or if it contains provisions related to the assignment of leases and rents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Retains all living signers on the existing Note as obligors. Except as otherwise provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 9206.2(d)(v), all Borrowers and any other signatory to the Security Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: must sign the modification agreement and all other required documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Contains a due-on-transfer provision if the existing Mortgage documents do not contain
elements.label: text

elements.text: such a provision (see Exhibit 78, Modification Due on Transfer Rider, for an example of
elements.label: text

elements.text: a due-on-transfer rider)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Does not have any secondary financing included in the UPB
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Does not provide any cash-out to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Except as set forth in Section 9206.1(c)(v), has an Escrow account (see Chapter 8201)
elements.label: text

elements.text: even if the existing Mortgage does not have an Escrow account
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Retains mortgage insurance coverage if the existing Mortgage has such coverage, and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: loss coverage percentage must remain the same
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. Contains an assignment of rents rider if the property is a 1-unit Investment Property or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2- to 4-unit property (see Exhibit 77 for an example of an assignment of rents rider)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9. Retains any existing credit enhancement, such as an indemnification agreement. (Note:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages subject to recourse are not eligible for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification®.) If
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer is not the provider of the credit enhancement, it must obtain written approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the institution providing the enhancement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 10. Contains a Modification Bankruptcy Disclosure Rider for a Borrower who has been
elements.label: text

elements.text: discharged from the Freddie Mac debt (see Exhibit 78A, Modification Bankruptcy
elements.label: text

elements.text: Disclosure Rider, for an example of the rider)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 11. Is a fully amortizing fixed-rate Mortgage. The Mortgage after modification must not be
elements.label: text

elements.text: an interest-only Mortgage, a bi-weekly Mortgage or a daily simple interest Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 12. Has flood insurance coverage if the property is located in an area that has been identified
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the Director of the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) as a Special
elements.label: text

elements.text: Flood Hazard Area (SFHA). The Servicer must determine if the area where the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: is located has been identified as a SFHA. This step must be taken even if the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: was not located in a SFHA when the Mortgage was originated. If the property is located
elements.label: text

elements.text: in a SFHA, then the Servicer must require that the Borrower purchase flood insurance as
elements.label: text

elements.text: required in Section 8202.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Other modification conditions and requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) EDR
elements.label: text

elements.text: Resolve automatically reports the below EDR default action codes when applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may, but are not required to, report specific Freddie Mac Flex Modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: activity to Freddie Mac as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ BF – “Standard Modification Trial Period.” Report default action code BF to
elements.label: text

elements.text: notify Freddie Mac that the Borrower has entered into a Trial Period for the Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac Flex Modification. Servicers may report this code along with the Trial Period
elements.label: text

elements.text: Plan Effective Date each month during the Trial Period. In addition, Servicers may
elements.label: text

elements.text: report this code if they elect to use the interim month option under the Trial Period
elements.label: text

elements.text: Plan or extend the Trial Period Plan to accommodate a Borrower’s bankruptcy filing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 9206.4(a)(ii) for information on the interim month option and Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9206.2(b)(ii) for information on extending a Borrower’s Trial Period Plan when the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower is in bankruptcy; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ HD – “Modification in Review.” Report default action code HD to notify Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac that the Borrower is being evaluated for a modification. Servicers may report
elements.label: text

elements.text: this code along with the date they began reviewing the Borrower for the modification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Report this code one time in the month following the month in which the evaluation
elements.label: text

elements.text: took place.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ HE – “Ineligible/Cancel Modification.” Report default action code HE to notify
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac that the Borrower is ineligible for a modification or the Trial Period has
elements.label: text

elements.text: been canceled. Servicers may report this code along with the date they made the
elements.label: text

elements.text: decision. Report this code one time in the month following the month in which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: decision took place.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) MI approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage is subject to mortgage insurance and approval and the Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification is not covered by delegation agreement, then the Servicer must obtain MI
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval of a modification before offering the Borrower a Trial Period Plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, if the Borrower cannot pay all amounts due plus the modification expenses,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must inquire if the MI will make an advance claim payment to pay all or part
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the amounts due.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Servicing Spread
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon modification, the Servicing Spread for Servicing a modified mortgage is equal to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the lesser of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The current Servicing Spread on the modified Mortgage, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 25 basis points per annum multiplied by the interest-bearing UPB. For Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: with a partial principal forbearance, the Servicing Spread is based on the interest￾bearing UPB.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Escrows
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must establish an Escrow account on the Mortgage if an Escrow account is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: currently maintained on the Mortgage, provided its establishment is not prohibited under
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable federal, State or local law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Prior to or during the Servicer’s determination of the Borrower’s eligibility for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification, the Servicer must perform an Escrow analysis in accordance with the Real
elements.label: text

elements.text: Estate Settlement Procedures Act (RESPA) and any applicable federal, State or local law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must then establish the Escrow account at the time the Trial Period Plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: becomes effective and provide any disclosures required by applicable federal, State or
elements.label: text

elements.text: local law within the time periods prescribed by such laws. In addition:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any advances previously made by the Servicer or any advances that will be made
elements.label: text

elements.text: during the Trial Period to pay property taxes or insurance premiums must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: capitalized in the modified UPB as long as they were or will be paid to third parties
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior to the Modification Effective Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For taxes and insurance premiums that are not yet due before the Modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Effective Date, the Servicer must determine the amount needed to establish the
elements.label: text

elements.text: escrow account (Escrow shortage) that, together with the monthly Escrow payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: included in the modified monthly Mortgage payment, will be sufficient to pay all
elements.label: text

elements.text: future taxes and insurance premiums when they fall due. If the Borrower is unable to
elements.label: text

elements.text: pay the Escrow shortage as a lump sum payment, then the Borrower must pay the
elements.label: text

elements.text: shortage as part of the monthly payment on the modified Mortgage (“Project Monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: Escrow Shortage Payments”) as set forth in Section 9206.3(b)(ii). This amount may
elements.label: text

elements.text: not be capitalized in the UPB of the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Once the Escrow account is established, the Borrower must continue to make
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly Escrow payments, even if the Borrower fails to comply with the Trial Period
elements.label: text

elements.text: Plan and the Mortgage subsequently reinstates.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Execution of documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must require all Borrowers and any other signatory to the Security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instrument to sign the modification agreement and all other required documents to
elements.label: text

elements.text: qualify for a modification except the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Borrower, co-Borrower or any signatory to the Security Instrument who is
elements.label: text

elements.text: deceased, as evidenced by a death certificate or an obituary or newspaper article
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting the death
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Borrower, co-Borrower or any signatory to the Security Instrument who is divorced
elements.label: text

elements.text: or legally separated from another party, as evidenced by a divorce decree signed by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the court or court filed separation agreement, except for the Borrower or co-Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: retaining possession and title to the property
elements.label: text
